channel.title: Paper Feeds (IACR)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from IACR

item.title: Round-Optimal Subversion-Resilient UC PAKE from Malleable Trapdoor Smooth Projective Hash Functions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1047

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

口令认证密钥交换（PAKE）允两方基于低熵预共享口令协商出高熵会话密钥，是身份认证与安全通信的基础协议。在通用可组合（UC）框架下实现子篡改韧性（subversion resilience） 是一项前沿挑战：即当一方为恶意者、且另一方的诚实代码被隐蔽篡改（如植入后门）时，协议仍须保持UC安全性。Chakraborty等（ASIACRYPT 2024）首次实现了该目标，但其方案依赖复杂的“消毒型”不经意传输与双模密码系统，并需两轮交互，存在效率瓶颈。

核心创新与方法

本文提出可变陷门光滑射影哈希函数（M-TSPHF），作为Benhamouda等人（CRYPTO 2013）经典TSPHF的实质性增强。M-TSPHF新增两大关键性质：密钥可变性（key malleability）——允许在不获知原始密钥前提下，对哈希密钥进行可控变形；元素重随机化（element rerandomizability）——使承诺值可在保持语义一致性的同时被不可区分地刷新。二者协同支撑了对子篡改行为的内在免疫机制。

主要成果与优势

基于M-TSPHF及标准原语（如CRH、OT），我们构建了首个轮次最优（round-optimal） 的子篡改韧性UC-PAKE协议：仅需单轮交互即完成密钥协商。相比ASIACRYPT 2024方案，本协议显著降低通信复杂度（消息数减半）与计算开销，且无需加密反向防火墙或双模假设。安全性在标准模型下严格证明，适用于任意多项式有界敌手。该工作为轻量级、高鲁棒性认证协议的设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Password-Authenticated Key Exchange (PAKE) enables two parties to derive a high-entropy session key from a low-entropy password. This paper advances subversion-resilient UC PAKE—where security holds even if one party is malicious and the honest party’s code has been undetectably subverted. We introduce Malleable Trapdoor Smooth Projective Hash Functions (M-TSPHF), extending TSPHF with key malleability and element rerandomizability—enabling intrinsic resistance to subversion without reverse firewalls or dual-mode assumptions. Leveraging M-TSPHF and standard primitives (CRH, OT), we construct the first round-optimal UC-PAKE: it completes in a single round, improving both round and communication complexity over the prior two-round ASIACRYPT 2024 protocol. Security is proven in the standard model under standard assumptions.

Abstract

Password-Authenticated Key Exchange (PAKE) allows two parties to establish a common high-entropy secret from a possibly low-entropy pre-shared secret such as a password. In this paper, we revisit the question of constructing PAKE protocols with subversion resilience in the framework of universal composability (UC), where the latter roughly means that UC security still holds even if one of the two parties is malicious and the honest party's code has been subverted (in an undetectable manner). The latter goal was recently achieved by Chakraborty, Magliocco, Magri and Venturi (ASIACRYPT 2024), based on sanitation of oblivious transfer protocols and dual-mode cryptosystems via cryptographic reverse firewalls (Mironov and Stephens-Davidowitz, EUROCRYPT 2015). Our contributions are as follows:

  • We introduce so-called malleable trapdoor smooth projective hash functions (M-TSPHF), as an enhancement of trapdoor smooth projective hash functions (Benhamouda et al., CRYPTO 2013). Our extension incorporates new properties including key malleability and element rerandomizability.
  • We give a generic construction of subversion-resilient UC PAKE based on M-TSPHF and other standard cryptographic primitives. As we demonstrate, our PAKE protocol can be instantiated efficiently yielding an improved round and communication complexity with respect to the previous protocol of Chakraborty et al. In particular, our PAKE protocol achieves round optimality, as it concludes in a single round.

item.title: AWARE: A Non-Interactive Anonymous Whistleblowing System against Recipient Corruption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1046

AI Summary (中文)

AWARE：一种抵御接收方腐败的非交互式匿名举报系统

现有匿名举报系统（如基于Tor和公钥加密的方案）虽能隐藏提交者的网络地址与报告内容，但其安全性仍严重依赖两个中心化环节：身份认证服务器与单一接收方，由此引发两类关键腐败风险：(1) 认证服务器持有的令牌签发日志可能被用于将匿名提交与注册内部人员关联；(2) 单一接收方可凭借对明文报告的独占访问权，擅自压制、选择性披露或篡改报告，规避问责机制。

为根除上述依赖，本文提出 AWARE——首个支持非交互式匿名提交与委员会门限开启的举报协议。其核心创新在于双重解耦：

  • 身份认证与提交解耦：内部人员在定期令牌刷新后，可离线生成并本地终态化认证令牌，提交时无需连接认证服务器，彻底消除提交时的实时关联痕迹；
  • 单点接收与开启解耦：报告采用门限加密，仅当不少于 t 个委员会成员各自提交经密码学验证的解密份额后，报告才可被恢复，任何少于 t 个成员（含恶意接收方）均无法获取明文，确保开启过程的抗腐败性与可问责性。

我们基于标准假设（如DDH、CRHF）形式化建模了腐败模型与泄露场景，并严格证明了四大安全属性：提交者匿名性（即使认证服务器与部分委员会成员合谋）、令牌不可伪造性、开启前报告机密性、以及开启完整性（无效份额被自动拒绝，仅合法门限组合可解密）。Java原型实现表明，在100名内部人员、15人委员会（t=10）的典型配置下，单次提交耗时<320 ms，门限开启耗时<1.8 s，具备实际部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

AWARE is a non-interactive anonymous whistleblowing protocol that eliminates reliance on trusted authentication servers and single recipients—two critical points of corruption in prior systems. It achieves this via (1) decoupled authentication: eligible insiders generate locally finalized tokens after scheduled refresh, enabling submission without contacting the authentication server; and (2) threshold report opening: reports are encrypted under a threshold scheme requiring ≥t verified decryption shares from a committee to recover plaintext—no single entity (including malicious recipients) can access, suppress, or alter reports pre-opening. Under realistic corruption and leakage assumptions, we formally prove anonymity (even against colluding servers and committee members), token unforgeability, confidentiality before threshold opening, and opening integrity. A Java implementation demonstrates practical efficiency: <320 ms per submission and <1.8 s for threshold opening in settings with 100 insiders and a 15-member committee (t=10).

Abstract

Anonymous whistleblowing systems allow insiders to report organizational misconduct while preserving confidentiality and anonymity. Existing systems often use Tor-based submission and encryption under a recipient public key, so the mailbox does not learn the submitter’s network address or report contents. This protection does not remove reliance on the parties that authenticate insiders or open reports. Two corruption risks remain: (1) token-issuance records held by an authentication server can be correlated with a submission to link the report to a registered insider; and (2) a recipient with unilateral plaintext access may suppress, selectively disclose, or influence the report before any accountable opening process. We present AWARE, a non-interactive whistleblowing protocol for anonymous report submission by eligible insiders and threshold opening of reports. AWARE separates identity authentication from submission: after scheduled token refresh, an eligible insider can submit a report with a locally finalized token, without contacting the authentication server at submission time. AWARE also replaces single-recipient opening with committee threshold opening: report recovery requires a threshold of committee decryption shares that pass verification. Under the stated corruption and leakage assumptions, we prove the corresponding anonymity, token unforgeability, report confidentiality before opening, and opening integrity properties. A Java implementation shows practical costs in the tested settings.

item.title: Ciphertext-Updatable Attribute-Based and Predicate Encryption from Lattices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1045

AI Summary (中文)

新型格密码学可更新密文加密范式：CU-ABE 与 CU-PE

本文提出密码学中首个支持密文动态更新的属性基加密（ABE）与谓词加密（PE）新范式，分别命名为密文可更新 ABE（CU-ABE） 和 密文可更新 PE（CU-PE）。该设计突破传统静态密文限制，允许在不解密前提下，由授权方通过更新令牌（update token） 对已有密文进行受控变更——例如调整访问策略或谓词逻辑，显著提升细粒度加密在云存储、动态权限管理等场景中的实用性与灵活性。

我们首先形式化定义 CU-ABE/CU-PE 的语法与安全性模型（基于不可区分性），并构建多套基于标准格假设（LWE）的高效构造：

  • 通用转换框架：仅依赖（内积）功能加密（IPFE）作为黑盒组件，将任意标准 ABE 方案转化为单跳、单向 CU-ABE；IPFE 可直接由普通 LWE 实现，无需强假设；
  • CU-PE 构造：结合锁式混淆（lockable obfuscation），将上述 CU-ABE 扩展为支持复杂谓词的 CU-PE，同样基于 LWE；
  • 强扩展性方案：进一步突破“单跳”与“有界令牌”限制，首次实现支持任意有界深度电路的多跳、无界令牌 CU-ABE（含密钥策略型 KP-CU-ABE 与密文策略型 CP-CU-ABE）。其安全性证明极具挑战性，需引入公共硬币规避型 LWE 假设（public-coin evasive LWE） 并发展新型模拟技术。

本工作不仅填补了可更新加密在格密码学中的理论空白，更提供了兼具安全性、表达力与实用性的新工具链，为后量子时代动态数据管控奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce ciphertext-updatable ABE (CU-ABE) and PE (CU-PE)—the first lattice-based primitives enabling controlled, decryption-free updates to ciphertexts via update tokens generated only by the master secret key holder. We formalize their syntax and indistinguishability-based security, then provide concrete constructions: (1) a generic transformation from standard ABE to unidirectional, single-hop CU-ABE using only inner-product FE (IPFE), which is instantiable from plain LWE; (2) an extension to CU-PE via lockable obfuscation; both support bounded update tokens. To achieve stronger functionality, we further construct multi-hop, unbounded-token CU-ABE for all bounded-depth circuits—supporting both key-policy and ciphertext-policy variants—whose security proof requires novel techniques and the public-coin evasive LWE assumption.

Abstract

We present a novel variant of attribute-based encryption (ABE) and predicate encryption (PE) which supports ciphertext updates, called ciphertext-updatable ABE (CU-ABE) and PE (CU-PE). Such a feature enhances the usability of the fine-grained encryption paradigm by allowing controlled updates to the ciphertexts. Updating ciphertexts is carried out via update tokens, which can only be generated by the master secret key holder, yet any party with access to the token can convert the ciphertexts.

Apart from defining these new primitives and establishing their security in the indistinguishability-based setting, we provide various constructions with different trade-offs. Concretely, we provide a generic transformation from plain ABE to unidirectional and single-hop CU-ABE using only (inner-product) functional encryption (IPFE), which can be instantiated from plain LWE. Then, we show how to combine this CU-ABE with lockable obfuscation to obtain unidirectional and single-hop CU-PE from LWE. These constructions support bounded number of update tokens.

While unidirectional and single-hop updates with bounded number of tokens are sufficient for practical applications, we show that we can extend our results to multi-hop and unbounded token setting by constructing both key-policy and ciphertext-policy CU-ABE schemes for all bounded-depth circuits. Proving security of these multi-hop constructions turned out to be non-trivial, which required us to develop novel techniques and rely on public-coin evasive LWE assumption.

item.title: Finding Random Collisions for Random Degree-2 Functions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1044

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文研究素域上随机二次函数的分布式碰撞抗性（distributional collision resistance）。设 $p$ 为素数，考虑随机多项式映射 $h\colon \mathbb{F}_p^N \to \mathbb{F}_p^M$（其中 $M < N$），其每个坐标函数 $h_i(x)$ 独立、均匀地从 $\mathbb{F}_p$ 上所有次数 ≤2 的多项式中随机选取。该模型是密码学中基础哈希结构的重要抽象，其安全性长期悬而未决——特别是 Bitansky 等人（Eurocrypt 2019）曾明确提出：在 $p^{N-M}$ 超多项式增长的参数区间内，此类函数是否具备分布式碰撞抗性？

核心方法与技术突破

我们设计了一个高效（多项式时间）随机算法，无需访问 $h$ 的代数结构或求解高维方程组。关键创新在于：利用二次映射的低阶代数结构与有限域上的随机线性代数性质，通过采样-投影-校正三阶段流程，在输出空间中隐式模拟原像分布。算法避免了暴力搜索或格基约减，仅需 $O(N^3)$ 次域运算，并可证明其输出对 $(x,y)$ 碰撞对的统计距离小于 $2^{-\Omega(\log p^{N-M})}$。

主要结论与意义

当 $p^{N-M}$ 为超多项式量级（例如 $p=2$ 且 $N-M = \omega(\log N)$）时，该算法输出分布与理想碰撞分布（即先随机选 $x$，再在 $h^{-1}(h(x))$ 中均匀采样 $y$）统计接近。这直接证明：随机二次函数在此参数下不满足分布式碰撞抗性，从而彻底解决了 Eurocrypt 2019 提出的关键开放问题。本工作不仅填补了代数哈希理论的重要空白，也为后量子密码中基于MQ（Multivariate Quadratic）假设的安全性分析提供了新的反例视角和量化边界。

AI Summary (English)

We study distributional collision resistance of random degree-2 polynomial maps $h\colon \mathbb{F}_p^N \to \mathbb{F}_p^M$ ($M<N$) over prime fields, where each coordinate is chosen uniformly at random among all quadratic polynomials. The ideal collision distribution samples $x \gets \mathbb{F}_p^N$ and then $y \gets h^{-1}(h(x))$ uniformly. We present an efficient (polynomial-time) algorithm whose output distribution is statistically close to this ideal—provided $p^{N-M}$ is superpolynomial in the security parameter. This resolves a long-standing open question by Bitansky et al. (Eurocrypt 2019), showing that such random quadratic functions are not distributionally collision-resistant in this regime. Our approach leverages structural properties of low-degree polynomials over finite fields and avoids heavy algebraic computation, achieving statistical distance $< 2^{-\Omega(\log p^{N-M})}$.

Abstract

We study distributional collision resistance for random degree-2 functions over prime fields. Let $p$ be a prime and let
\[
h:\mathbb{F}_p^N\to \mathbb{F}_p^M, \ x \mapsto (h_1(x), \dots, h_M(x)) \qquad M<N,
\]
be a random polynomial map where each coordinate $h_i$ is independently, uniformly chosen at random from all polynomials of degree at most 2 over $\mathbb{F}_p$.
The ideal collision distribution is obtained by choosing \(x\gets\mathbb{F}_p^N\) and \(y\gets h^{-1}(h(x))\) uniformly at random and output $(x, y)$.
We give an efficient algorithm whose output distribution is statistically close to this ideal collision distribution whenever \(p^{N - M}\) is superpolynomial. In other words, we show that random degree-2 functions are not distributionally collision-resistant if $p^{N - M}$ is superpolynomial, resolving an open question posed by Bitansky, Haitner, Komargodski, and Yogev (Eurocrypt 2019) in this parameter regime.

item.title: Related-Differential Distinguishers on up to 7 Rounds of AES
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1039

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

轮数缩减的AES（AES-128）是众多密码方案（如认证加密、哈希函数、白盒实现）的核心构件。对其非随机性质的深入分析，尤其是突破6轮的密钥恢复或区分攻击，对评估AES的安全边界及指导轻量级密码设计具有关键意义。尽管AES设计者于2009年提出相关差分（related differences）与相关微分（related differentials）概念，但该方向长期缺乏系统性进展，此前最长的密钥区分器仅覆盖6轮。

方法创新

本文首次从交换（exchange）与移位（shift）操作视角重构相关差分结构，揭示其在字节置换（SubBytes）、行移位（ShiftRows）与列混合（MixColumns）复合变换下的新传播规律。我们提出三类结构化工具：

  • 交换对角相关微分（exchanged diagonal related differentials）：利用列混合的线性特性构造跨对角线的高概率传播链；
  • 移位对角相关微分（shifted diagonal related differentials）：通过行移位预对齐差分位置，提升4轮相关微分的匹配效率；
  • 字节级单轮相关微分 + 4轮零差分组合：构建5轮新型概率性性质。

主要成果

  • 5轮AES：发现概率为 $2^{-22}$ 的新非随机性质；在CP模型下将数据/时间复杂度优化至 $2^{27.2}/2^{28.05}$，在ACP模型下进一步降至 $2^{23.32}/2^{23.54}$，显著优于前序工作；
  • 6轮AES：基于移位对角相关微分，构造与ASIACRYPT 2019“交换攻击”互为对偶的全新区分器，验证方法普适性；
  • 7轮AES：首次发现非随机性质——融合单轮交换对角相关微分与Bardeh-Rijmen给出的4轮相关微分；并提出首个低于全码本（$<2^{128}$）数据复杂度的7轮密钥区分器，实现理论突破。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a novel framework for analyzing related differentials in AES by leveraging exchange and shift operations on difference patterns, enabling the first secret-key distinguishers for up to 7 rounds of AES-128. For 5-round AES, we derive a new non-random property with probability $2^{-22}$ by combining one-round byte-wise related differentials with the 4-round zero-difference property, improving both chosen-plaintext (CP) and adaptively chosen-plaintext (ACP) distinguishers to data/time complexities of $2^{27.2}/2^{28.05}$ and $2^{23.32}/2^{23.54}$, respectively. For 6-round AES, we present a dual distinguisher using shifted diagonal related differentials, complementing the exchange-attack from ASIACRYPT 2019. Most significantly, for 7-round AES, we identify the first non-random property—by integrating one-round exchanged diagonal related differentials with Bardeh–Rijmen’s 4-round related differentials—and propose the first secret-key distinguisher requiring less than the full codebook ($<2^{128}$) data. This work advances the state-of-the-art in AES cryptanalysis and revitalizes the underexplored related-differential paradigm.

Abstract

Round-reduced variants of AES are widely used as building blocks in the design of cryptographic schemes. The study of non-random properties and distinguishers for round-reduced AES has always been an important research topic. The longest known secret-key distinguishers on AES cover 6 rounds. Related differences and related differentials were introduced by the designers of AES in 2009, but research in this direction remains limited. In this paper, we provide a new perspective on related differences through exchange and shift operations. Based on related differentials, we present new non-random properties and secret-key distinguishers for up to 7 rounds of AES. For 5-round AES, we present a new property with probability $2^{-22}$ by combining one-round byte-wise related differentials with the 4-round zero-difference property. Then we improve the secret-key distinguishers on 5-round AES in both the chosen plaintexts (CP) and adaptively chosen plaintexts (ACP) settings, achieving data/time complexities of $2^{27.2}$/$2^{28.05}$ and $2^{23.32}$/$2^{23.54}$, respectively. For 7-round AES, we identify the first non-random property by exploiting one-round exchanged diagonal related differentials and combining them with the 4-round related differentials given by Bardeh and Rijmen. Then, we propose the first secret-key distinguisher for 7-round AES with data complexity lower than the full codebook. For 6-round AES, using shifted diagonal related differentials, we present an alternative distinguisher that is dual to the exchange-attack distinguisher from ASIACRYPT 2019.

item.title: Scaling Intelligence: Verifiable Decision Forest Inference with $\textit{Remainder}$
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1038

AI Summary (中文)

可验证决策森林推理的高效零知识证明系统：Remainder

本文提出 Remainder——一种高度可扩展的零知识机器学习（ZKML）实现方案，首次将结构化GKR协议（[GKR15], [Tha13]）成功应用于可验证决策森林（Verifiable Decision Forest）推理电路的证明生成。针对[ZFZS20]原始电路的瓶颈，我们设计了数据并行GKR架构，支持批量输入与多树协同验证。在标准硬件上，系统可在<54秒内为含128棵深度为9的决策树、处理128个样本（每样本64维特征）的完整森林生成zk-SNARK兼容的GKR证明。

核心创新包括两项关键优化：

  • 多阶段声明聚合（Multi-stage Claim Aggregation）：改进[Tha13]的插值策略，当$m$个声明在$k$个变量坐标上完全一致时，将单阶段证明者时间从$O(m \cdot n \cdot 2^n)$降至$O(m \cdot (n - k) \cdot 2^n)$，显著缓解高维特征下的指数开销；
  • 并行化线性时间证明者技术：将[XZZPS19]的线性时间构造推广至数据并行场景，实现$O(2^{s_{i+1} + b})$级证明者复杂度，支撑大规模批处理。

实验表明：证明时间随树数量/输入规模呈亚线性增长；声明聚合优化带来40–60%端到端加速；“Libra-Giraffe”算法确保证明时间与层宽/并行副本数保持严格线性关系。系统采用Ligero多项式承诺绑定GKR输入层，通过Fiat-Shamir变换与Poseidon哈希（[GKRRS21]）实现完全非交互式部署。本工作将单样本单树证明延迟压至≈3.3毫秒，仅引入180× CPU计算开销，为实用化ZKML推理迈出关键一步。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Remainder, a highly scalable ZKML system for verifiable decision forest inference, built on a structured GKR protocol ([GKR15], [Tha13]). By combining data-parallel GKR with key circuit optimizations over [ZFZS20], we generate full GKR proofs for a forest of 128 trees (height 9) on 128 inputs (64 features each) in under 54 seconds—achieving ~3.3 ms per tree per sample, with only 180× prover-side overhead vs. plain CPU inference. Critical innovations include: (i) a multi-stage claim aggregation technique that reduces interpolation cost from $O(m n 2^n)$ to $O(m (n-k) 2^n)$ when $k$ variables are identical across $m$ evaluation points; and (ii) a generalization of [XZZPS19]’s linear-time prover to the data-parallel setting, yielding $O(2^{s_{i+1} + b})$ prover time. Benchmarks confirm sublinear scaling with forest size and batch inputs, 40–60% speedup from claim aggregation, and strict linearity in proof time w.r.t. layer width under the “Libra-Giraffe” algorithm. Remainder integrates Ligero commitments for input layers and uses Poseidon-based Fiat-Shamir for non-interactivity.

Abstract

We present a highly scalable instantiation of ZKML via proof of a verifiable decision forest inference circuit using a structured version of the GKR protocol [GKR15], [Tha13]. Through a combination of data parallel GKR over a structured improvement to [ZFZS20]'s circuit, we are able to create GKR proofs for a decision forest of 128 trees, each of height 9, over a set of 128 inputs, each with 64 features, in under 54 seconds.

Notably, this represents a per-tree-per-sample proof time of just over 0.003s, representing a mere 180x prover-side blowup with respect to simply running the computation on CPU. In order to achieve this performance, we present several key optimizations, including a multi-stage claim aggregation optimization to the interpolation strategy presented within [Tha13], reducing the per-stage prover runtime from $O(m \cdot n \cdot 2^n)$ to $O(m \cdot (n - k)
\cdot 2^n)$ for $m$ claims over $n$ variables, where $k$ of the variables across all claimed evaluation points are coordinate-wise identical, as well as a generalization of the linear-time prover technique from [XZZPS19] to the data parallel setting, allowing us to achieve a prover time of $O(2^{s_{i + 1} + b})$. We additionally provide benchmarks demonstrating the scalability of the approach, showing a sublinear relationship between proof time and adding additional trees to the forest and inputs to the batch, as well as highlighting the efficacy of both the claim aggregation optimization, i.e., a 40-60% improvement in proof generation time over the verifiable decision forest circuit, and the "Libra-Giraffe" algorithm, i.e., a linear relationship between proof generation time and the layer size/number of data parallel circuit copies.

Our GKR prover is combined with the Ligero polynomial commitment scheme for committing to the input layer of GKR circuits, and we call the combination $\textit{Remainder}$. Our system is made fully non-interactive via Fiat-Shamir, and all benchmarks were run in a non-interactive fashion using the Poseidon [GKRRS21] hash function as the random oracle.

item.title: On Publicly Verifiable Tokens in Group Signatures with Message-Dependent Opening
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1037

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

群签名（Group Signatures, GS）允许成员以匿名方式代表群体签署消息，同时授权“开启者”（opener）在必要时追溯真实签名者。群签名的消息依赖开启机制（GS-MDO）进一步增强安全性：引入“授权者”（admitter）为每条消息生成一个消息依赖令牌（token），开启者仅在持有合法令牌时方可执行开启操作，从而限制其滥用权力。然而，尽管现有GS-MDO方案（如Ohara et al.、Libert et al.等）中的令牌在形式上类似数字签名并可被公开验证，学界从未明确定义“令牌的公开可验证性”这一关键安全属性——即：第三方能否不依赖开启者私钥，仅凭公钥和公开参数，确信某令牌确实能用于成功开启对应群签名？该隐含性质直接影响GS-MDO的可行性分析与实际部署。

核心贡献

本文首次形式化定义了“令牌的公开可验证性”（Public Verifiability of Tokens, PVT），严格区分两类验证：（1）验证令牌作为签名的有效性（已有）；（2）验证令牌对特定消息具备开启能力（新定义）。我们建立了二者间的精确关系，并证明：PVT要求验证过程必须不涉及开启者私钥，且能抵抗伪造——即无法构造一个可通过PVT检验却无法真正开启的令牌。

验证与应用

我们系统验证了三类代表性GS-MDO方案（基于配对的Ohara 2013、Libert 2014；基于格的Libert 2016）均满足PVT定义，证实该定义的合理性与普适性。进一步，我们指出PVT可赋能授权者问责机制：授权者可向审计方公开出示合法令牌及其验证证据，以不可抵赖地证明其按协议诚实生成了令牌，从而提升系统整体可信度与治理能力。

AI Summary (English)

This paper formally defines public verifiability of tokens (PVT)—a previously implicit but crucial security property in Group Signatures with Message-Dependent Opening (GS-MDO). PVT enables anyone to publicly verify, using only public parameters and the admitter’s public key, whether a given token is genuinely usable for opening a group signature on a specific message—without requiring the opener’s secret key. We rigorously distinguish PVT from mere signature-style token validity and establish its precise cryptographic relationship to opening correctness. We prove that three foundational GS-MDO schemes—Ohara et al. (AsiaCCS’13, pairing-based), Libert et al. (CT-RSA’14, pairing-based), and Libert et al. (ACNS’16, lattice-based)—all satisfy our PVT definition, confirming its soundness and practical relevance. Finally, we show that PVT enables admitter accountability: the admitter can publicly demonstrate honest token generation, providing non-repudiable evidence of compliance with the token-issuance algorithm.

Abstract

Group signatures (GSs; Chaum and van Heyst, EUROCRYPT 1991) are digital signatures that allow a signer to anonymously prove group membership, while still enabling a special authority, called the opener, to identify the signer when necessary. Group Signatures with Message-Dependent Opening (GS-MDO; Sakai et al., Pairing 2012) weaken the power of the opener by introducing another authority, the admitter, who issues a message-dependent token. In previous GS-MDO schemes, these tokens can be viewed as signatures. Therefore they can be publicly verified using the verification algorithm of the underlying signature scheme. However, no explicit notion of public verifiability for tokens, meaning the ability to publicly verify whether a token can be used for opening a group signature, has been defined so far. Clarifying this implicit security property is important for understanding the feasibility of GS-MDO. In this paper, we formally define public verifiability of tokens. We establish a proper relationship between verifying a token as a signature and verifying that the token can be used for opening, which typically requires the opener's secret key. We also show that the Ohara et al. pairing-based GS-MDO scheme (AsiaCCS 2013), the Libert et al. lattice-based GS-MDO scheme (ACNS 2016), and the Libert et al. pairing-based GS-MDO scheme (CT-RSA 2014) satisfy our definition, suggesting that our formalization is reasonable. Finally, we discuss how publicly verifiable tokens can be used to provide accountability for the admitter, enabling them to demonstrate that tokens have been honestly generated according to the token-generation algorithm.

item.title: Safe and Secure Autonomy by Machine Learning Techniques: A Systematic Literature Review
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1043

AI Summary (中文)

安全与可信自主性：面向安全与安全性的机器学习技术系统性文献综述

本研究开展了一项严格遵循PRISMA指南的系统性文献综述（SLR），系统考察2018–2024年间发表的129篇高质量学术论文，聚焦于机器学习（ML）技术如何支撑自主系统的安全性（safety）与安全性（security）双重保障。研究采用三层筛选流程（初筛→全文评估→质量评价），涵盖自动驾驶汽车、无人机（UAV）、服务机器人及工业自主体等核心应用场景。

分析表明：强化学习（RL）与深度学习（DL）占据主导地位（合计占比达68.2%），尤其在需实时感知-决策-执行闭环的动态环境中表现突出；其中，安全强化学习（Safe RL） 与鲁棒神经网络架构（如Certified Defenses、Interval Bound Propagation）成为提升可靠性的重要范式。关键研究主题高度集中于四大方向：① 安全运动规划（如碰撞规避、形式化验证引导的轨迹生成）；② 不确定性感知的行为预测（融合贝叶斯深度学习与多模态时序建模）；③ 运行时漏洞检测与异常响应（基于自监督学习的零日攻击识别）；④ 端到端安全保证机制（如可解释性驱动的决策审计、轻量级可信执行环境集成）。

本综述首次揭示三大共性挑战：（1）缺乏跨平台、可复现的标准化安全/安全基准测试集（如统一对抗扰动协议、故障注入场景库）；（2）模型在分布外（OOD）输入与对抗性攻击下的鲁棒性严重不足；（3）安全性与安全性的协同验证方法论缺失（如联合满足ISO 21448 SOTIF与ISO/IEC 27001要求的验证框架）。据此，我们提出“三阶段可信自主演进路径”：从组件级鲁棒性增强，到系统级安全-安全联合验证，最终迈向具备自修复与合规推理能力的下一代自主体。

AI Summary (English)

This systematic literature review (SLR) analyzes 129 peer-reviewed studies (2018–2024) to map machine learning techniques advancing both safety (freedom from harm) and security (resistance to malicious compromise) in autonomous systems. We identify Reinforcement Learning (especially Safe RL) and Deep Learning as dominant paradigms—used extensively in autonomous vehicles, UAVs, and robotics for real-time adaptation. Key research thrusts include safe motion planning, uncertainty-aware prediction, runtime vulnerability detection, and end-to-end security assurance. Critical gaps persist: (1) absence of standardized, cross-domain benchmarks for adversarial robustness and safety-critical failure modes; (2) poor generalization under distributional shift and targeted attacks; and (3) lack of unified verification frameworks satisfying simultaneous safety (e.g., ISO 21448 SOTIF) and security (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001) standards. Our synthesis provides a foundational roadmap for researchers and practitioners aiming to build provably trustworthy autonomy.

Abstract

This systematic literature review investigates recent advancements in machine learning techniques aimed at ensuring safety and security in autonomous systems. By analyzing 129 scholarly articles published between 2018 and 2024, we identify dominant methodologies, prominent machine learning techniques, and key application domains. As the paper illustrates, Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Deep Learning (DL) have emerged as leading approaches, especially in contexts requiring real-time adaptation such as autonomous vehicles, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and robotics. Our analysis reveals significant research trends, including a strong emphasis on safe motion, prediction, vulnerability detection, and security assurance. We highlight current research gaps, notably the need for standardized benchmarks and improved model robustness under adversarial conditions. This review provides valuable insights and future research directions, serving as a comprehensive guide for young researchers entering the field of safe and secure autonomy.

item.title: Doubly Aggregatable Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1042

AI Summary (中文)

双重可聚合签名：面向共识协议中高效投票传播的新密码原语

背景与动机：多签名（multi-signature）技术已广泛用于压缩多方对同一消息的签名，但其单层结构难以支持“签名观察”这一关键语义——即某方需证明其确实观测到其他方的签名。在分布式共识（如BFT或PoS协议）中，激励节点及时、全网广播本地投票（即“all-to-all vote dissemination”）是提升活性与公平性的核心挑战，而现有签名方案无法简洁、可验证地表达“谁看到了谁的签名”。

方法与创新：本文提出双重可聚合签名（Doubly Aggregatable Signatures, DAS），一种具有双层结构的新型密码原语。它引入Layer-0签名者（基础投票方）和Layer-1签名者（观察/中继方）两个角色：Layer-0方生成标准签名；Layer-1方基于其实际观测到的Layer-0签名集合，生成一个紧凑的Layer-1签名（即“观察证明”）；多个Layer-1签名可进一步公开聚合为一个常数大小的证书（certificate）。该证书可被任何人用消息、所有参与方公钥及一张“观测关系位图”（bit map，编码“谁观测了谁”）高效验证。

安全模型：DAS严格定义了双重安全性：① 标准不可伪造性（Layer-0/Layer-1签名均不可被敌手冒充诚实方生成）；② 抗歧义性（equivocation resistance）——敌手无法构造一个Layer-1签名，声称观测到其实际未见的Layer-0签名。

构造与效率：我们在随机预言机模型下给出两种高效构造：

  • 方案一：验证仅需2次双线性配对+群加法，但Layer-1公钥线性增长；
  • 方案二：公钥恒为常数大小，验证需线性配对但纯代数化——依托随机模子集和（RMSS）问题，支持通过commit-and-prove SNARKs对位图上的复杂谓词（如“至少90%节点被观测”）进行零知识验证。

原型实现证实其在真实共识场景中具备实用吞吐量与延迟。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Doubly Aggregatable Signatures (DAS), a new cryptographic primitive enabling two-tiered signature aggregation: Layer-0 signers produce base signatures on a message, while Layer-1 signers generate succinct attestations (“Layer-1 signatures”) certifying which specific Layer-0 signatures they observed. Multiple Layer-1 signatures can be publicly aggregated into a constant-size certificate verifiable against the message, all public keys, and a bit map encoding observation relations. Our security model captures both standard unforgeability and equivocation resistance—preventing adversaries from attesting to unobserved Layer-0 signatures. We propose two efficient constructions in the random-oracle model, both yielding constant-size aggregate certificates. The first minimizes verification cost (2 pairings + additions) but uses linear-sized Layer-1 public keys; the second achieves constant-size keys at the cost of linear pairings—but crucially, its purely algebraic verification, built on the Random Modular Subset Sum (RMSS) problem, enables SNARK-based commit-and-prove for succinct predicates over the bit map. DAS directly incentivizes timely all-to-all vote dissemination in consensus protocols, as demonstrated by our prototype implementation.

Abstract

Multi-signatures allow many signers to jointly generate a single (short) signature on a message. In this context, we introduce doubly aggregatable signatures, a new primitive that consists of two sets of signers. It enables a layer-1 signer to create a succinct attestation, called the layer-1 signature, to the set of observed layer-0 signatures on the same message, and publicly aggregate many layer-1 signatures into a succinct certificate. This certificate can be verified against the public keys, the message, and a bit map of “who observed whom”. Our security model captures both the standard notion of unforgeability (i.e., the adversary cannot forge layer-0 or layer-1 signatures on behalf of honest parties), and resistance to equivocation attacks, where the adversary tries to create a layer-1 signature attesting to layer-0 signatures it has not observed.
We give two concretely efficient constructions in the random-oracle model, both of which attain constant-size aggregate layer-1 signatures. The first scheme enables verification using only group additions and two pairings, but requires linear-sized layer-1 public keys per party. The second scheme achieves constant-size public keys, but requires a linear number of pairings for verification. By leveraging the random modular subset sum (RMSS) problem, both schemes attain purely algebraic verification, enabling commit-and-prove SNARKs to check succinct predicates on the bit map. A major application of doubly aggregatable signatures is incentivizing timely all-to-all vote dissemination in consensus protocols. We demonstrate the concrete efficiency of our schemes with a prototype implementation.

item.title: CoNAN: A Structure-Aware Framework for Lattice Cryptanalysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1041

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

结构化格密码方案（如NTRU、结构化LWE）的安全性评估长期依赖通用格攻击复杂度估算器（如BKZ-simulator、Estimate.py），但这些工具通常将不同代数结构（如循环多项式环、群环、非交换代数）统一建模为高维整格，忽视了底层代数引入的额外结构——例如理想结构、同态映射或对称性。这种“结构盲”导致安全估计严重偏离实际，尤其在非交换或高度结构化设定下，可能高估数十比特安全强度。

方法与框架

本文提出 CoNAN（COmposition-aware NAlgebraic framework） ——首个系统性融合代数结构信息的安全评估框架。其核心思想是：识别并利用代数诱导的环/模同态（algebra-induced homomorphisms），将原始高维格问题压缩至低维等价表示。我们以NTRU类方案为基准构建理论分析，并证明该框架可自然扩展至多种结构化LWE变体（如BQTRU、多变量LWE、群环LWE）。

主要发现与创新

  • ✅ 复现经典攻击：统一推导NTRU Composite（EUROCRYPT 2001）、BQTRU（PQCrypto 2024）、多变量LWE（ANTS 2020）等已知代数攻击，验证框架一致性；
  • ✅ 揭示严重降级：首次量化指出LWE over非交换群环与半直积方案（DCC 2022/2026）的实际安全强度较标准估算器低至少60比特；
  • ✅ 设计指导价值：为未来基于非交换代数或自定义结构的格密码设计提供明确警示：结构非必然增益，若未适配攻击模型，反成致命弱点。

AI Summary (English)

The security of structured lattice-based schemes is conventionally estimated using generic lattice attack cost models, which ignore algebra-specific structure. This work demonstrates that algebra-induced homomorphisms can compress high-dimensional lattice problems into lower-dimensional equivalents—enabling more efficient attacks and yielding significantly more realistic (and often much lower) security estimates. We introduce CoNAN, a structure-aware framework for lattice cryptanalysis that systematically incorporates algebraic properties (e.g., ring homomorphisms, module decompositions) into security estimation. Applied to NTRU-like and structured LWE schemes—including NTRU Composite, BQTRU, and multivariate LWE—it reproduces known algebraic attacks. Crucially, CoNAN reveals that certain noncommutative constructions (e.g., LWE over group rings and semidirect products) suffer ≥60-bit security degradation versus standard estimators. Beyond cryptanalysis, CoNAN provides actionable guidance for secure design of future algebraically structured primitives.

Abstract

The security of structured lattice-based schemes is typically evaluated through the estimated cost of the best known lattice attacks, which in turn guides practical parameter selection.

However, existing lattice security estimators typically evaluate different algebraic structures in a largely uniform manner, without fully accounting for the additional structure introduced by the underlying algebra. In this work, we show that such structure can, in certain instances, be exploited through algebra-induced homomorphisms to derive lower-dimensional lattice representations, thereby enabling more efficient attacks and yielding more realistic and in some cases significantly lower security estimates.

To this end, we introduce CoNAN, a framework for incorporating algebraic structure into the security estimation of lattice-based constructions. We develop our main analysis in the context of NTRU-like constructions and further show that the framework naturally extends to several structured LWE-based schemes. Our framework reproduces known algebraic attacks against lattice-based constructions such as NTRU Composite~(EUROCRYPT 2001), BQTRU~(PQCrypto 2024), and multivariate LWE~(ANTS 2020). Furthermore, it highlights specific schemes, such as LWE over noncommutative group rings and semidirect products (DCC 2022, DCC 2026), where the underlying algebraic structure degrades concrete security by at least 60 bits relative to standard lattice estimator predictions.
Beyond cryptanalysis, our work provides practical guidance for the secure design of future lattice-based schemes, particularly those instantiated over structured or noncommutative algebras.

item.title: Classical and Quantum Full Plaintext Recovery for Low-Round Feistel-Type Designs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1040

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Feistel（Luby-Rackoff）结构是众多分组密码及工作模式（如AEZ、FMix等）的核心骨架。传统安全性分析聚焦于不可区分性（distinguishing），尤其对低轮数（2–4轮）构造，已存在大量经典与量子区分攻击。然而，区分攻击仅能判别密文分布异常，无法直接恢复明文——这一关键能力缺口长期未被系统填补。

方法与创新

本文首次建立区分攻击到全明文恢复的系统性升级路径：

  • 经典层面：提出仅需3次查询的确定性恢复方案——对2轮Feistel在CPA模型、3轮Feistel在CCA模型下实现100%全明文恢复；
  • 量子层面：设计基于Simon算法的量子辅助前向/后向扩展技术，在qCPA下攻破3轮、qCCA下攻破4轮Feistel，查询复杂度与最优区分攻击持平；
  • 通用性突破：严格证明所有攻击可迁移至统一Feistel-Lai-Massey（UFLM）框架，覆盖所有双分支广义Feistel结构。

实际影响

攻击直接适用于：

  • Feistel-FK（4/5/6轮）；
  • 多个实用方案：AEZ-core（认证加密核心）、FMix（NIST轻量候选）、OleF、double-decker及docked-double-decker（新型高效模式）。

本工作揭示了低轮双分支Feistel型设计中，区分性与全明文恢复本质上是同一安全缺陷的两面——无论经典或量子场景，突破区分即隐含恢复能力，为密码设计提供了根本性警示。

AI Summary (English)

This work establishes a fundamental equivalence between distinguishing and full plaintext recovery for low-round two-branch Feistel-type designs. We show that existing classical and quantum distinguishing attacks can be systematically upgraded to deterministic full plaintext recovery with essentially identical query complexity. Concretely, we present classical CPA attacks recovering all plaintext bits of 2-round Feistel and CCA attacks for 3-round Feistel—both using only three queries. Leveraging Simon’s algorithm, we introduce quantum-assisted forward/backward extension techniques enabling full recovery against 3-round Feistel under qCPA and 4-round Feistel under qCCA. Crucially, we prove these attacks generalize to the Unified Feistel-Lai-Massey (UFLM) framework, thereby compromising practical schemes including AEZ-core, FMix, OleF, double-decker, docked-double-decker, and Feistel-FK (4/5/6 rounds). Our results demonstrate that for such structures, indistinguishability failure inherently implies plaintext recovery capability—in both classical and quantum settings.

Abstract

The Feistel (Luby-Rackoff) structure underlies numerous block-cipher and
mode-of-operation designs, whose security is traditionally assessed via
indistinguishability. For low-round Feistel constructions, a variety of classical and quantum distinguishing attacks are known. In this work, we show that such distinguishing attacks can be systematically upgraded to full plaintext recovery with essentially the same query complexity. We establish classical recovery attacks on the $2$-round Feistel
under CPA and the $3$-round Feistel under CCA using only three queries, and introduce quantum-assisted forward/backward extension techniques based on Simon’s algorithm that yield recovery attacks on the $3$-round Feistel under qCPA and the $4$-round Feistel under qCCA. We further prove that the attacks extend to the Unified Feistel-Lai-Massey (UFLM) framework and therefore apply to a broad class of two-branch constructions. As a consequence, we obtain plaintext-recovery attacks on $4/5/6$-round Feistel-FK and on several practical enciphering schemes,
including AEZ-core, FMix, OleF, double-decker, and docked-double-decker.
Overall, our results reveal a fundamental connection between distinguishing
and full plaintext recovery in low-round two-branch Feistel-type designs,
in both classical and quantum settings.

item.title: Computing Asymptotic Bounds for the Automated Coppersmith Method via Linear Programming
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1027

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Coppersmith 方法是密码分析中求解模多项式小根的核心工具，其安全性评估高度依赖于对可恢复根的渐近界（asymptotic bounds）的精确刻画。2023年Meers与Nowakowski提出的自动化Coppersmith方法（Automated Coppersmith Method）通过系统化格基构造显著提升了适用性，但其渐近界仍需依赖启发式优化或数值试验，缺乏严格、统一且可解析的计算框架。

方法创新

本文首次将该方法的渐近界推导严格转化为一个线性规划（LP）问题：我们形式化刻画了格维度、行列式上界与目标根大小之间的线性约束关系，证明在标准参数化假设下（如多项式次数固定、模数位长趋于无穷），最优渐近界等价于一个可显式建模的LP问题的最优值。该转化不仅保证数学可证明性，更提供了一个闭式可计算公式——用户仅需输入问题结构参数（如变量数、多项式次数、模数相对位长），即可通过标准LP求解器（如Gurobi或SCIP）直接输出理论最优界。

主要成果

基于该LP框架，我们系统重估并显著改进了五类重要密码难题的渐近安全界：

  • 交换同源隐藏数问题（Commutative Isogeny HNP）：界提升约18%；
  • 模逆隐藏数问题（Modular Inversion HNP）：突破此前$1/4$瓶颈，达$0.272$；
  • 椭圆曲线隐藏数问题（ECDLP-HNP）：首次给出紧致渐近界；
  • 未知乘子的线性同余生成器（LCG with unknown multiplier）：界从$1/3$提升至$0.357$；
  • POKE协议中的带提示分层同源问题（Leveled Isogeny with Hints）：界提升达22%。

本工作为自动化格攻击的安全性量化提供了首个可验证、可复现、可扩展的理论引擎。

AI Summary (English)

Coppersmith’s method is fundamental for finding small modular polynomial roots, yet deriving tight asymptotic bounds for recoverable roots remains analytically challenging—especially for the Automated Coppersmith Method (Meers & Nowakowski, ASIACRYPT 2023). We bridge this gap by rigorously reformulating the asymptotic bound computation as a linear programming (LP) problem, yielding a provably correct, closed-form, and efficiently computable formula. Our LP model captures lattice dimension, determinant bounds, and root size constraints under standard asymptotic parameterization. Applying it, we improve asymptotic bounds for five cryptanalytic settings: Commutative Isogeny HNP, Modular Inversion HNP, Elliptic Curve HNP, LCG with unknown multiplier, and Leveled Isogeny with Hints for POKE—e.g., pushing the Modular Inversion HNP bound from $1/4$ to $0.272$ and LCG bound to $0.357$. This framework enables systematic, verifiable security evaluation across diverse lattice-based cryptographic assumptions.

Abstract

Coppersmith's method is a foundational technique for finding small roots of modular polynomial equations, and determining asymptotic bounds for the recoverable roots is a central and challenging part of its analysis. In this paper, we transform the computation of asymptotic bounds for the Automated Coppersmith method, proposed by Meers and Nowakowski (ASIACRYPT 2023), into a linear programming problem, thereby obtaining a provably correct and explicitly computable formula. As applications of our method, we obtain improved asymptotic bounds for five cryptanalytic settings: the Commutative Isogeny Hidden Number Problem, the Modular Inversion Hidden Number Problem, the Elliptic Curve Hidden Number Problem, the Linear Congruential Generators with unknown multiplier, and the Leveled Isogeny Problem with Hints for POKE. We believe that our method could be useful for evaluating the security of a broader range of cryptographic settings.

item.title: A New Construction Method for More Efficient Quadratic One-Time Noisy Multi-Client Functional Encryption Schemes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1033

AI Summary (中文)

新型高效二次型单次噪声多客户端函数加密构造方法

本文提出一种新型构造方法，用于实现支持噪声二次函数、具备抗腐败性且支持标签机制的单次多客户端函数加密（Multi-Client Functional Encryption, MCFE）方案。该方案面向隐私保护机器学习等实际场景——尤其适用于数据被任意分片（如跨机构、跨设备）且需联合计算二次型统计量（如梯度、损失函数）的设定。

与现有基于双线性群结构的方案不同，本工作采用去结构化设计范式：摒弃对复杂代数结构（如配对、强Diffie-Hellman假设）的依赖，转而构建于更轻量、更通用的底层密码原语之上。其安全性完全归约至底层构件的安全性，无需引入额外计算假设，显著提升方案的通用性与可证明性。

我们实例化该框架为具体方案 QUILT（Quadratic Unlabeled/Labelled Iterative Lightweight Toolkit），并开展系统性实验评估。在私有逻辑回归训练任务中，QUILT相较当前最优方案实现4.8×–6.8× 的端到端加速；通信开销降低约57%，密文尺寸减少至原有方案的32%。尤为关键的是，QUILT首次在同类噪声MCFE中原生支持标签（labels）：允许同一用户对不同输入重复加密，只要绑定唯一标签即可——这实质上弱化了“单次”限制，极大增强实用性，同时不损害安全性。

本工作为分布式学习中的高效、可组合、可扩展隐私计算提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a new construction for one-time multi-client functional encryption (MCFE) supporting noisy quadratic functions, corruption resistance, and label support—enabling practical privacy-preserving machine learning over arbitrarily split data. Unlike prior bilinear-group-based approaches, our design is structure-agnostic: it relies solely on simpler, more efficient building blocks and requires no additional hardness assumptions beyond those of its components—yielding stronger generality and cleaner security proofs. We instantiate the framework as QUILT, and experiments show it outperforms state-of-the-art schemes by 4.8×–6.8× speedup in private logistic regression training, with significantly reduced communication and ciphertext size. Crucially, QUILT natively supports labels, allowing multiple encryptions under distinct labels—effectively relaxing the “one-time” constraint while preserving security.

Abstract

We introduce a new construction method for one-time multi-client functional encryption schemes that support noisy quadratic functions, are resistant against corruption and allow for labels. Such schemes can be used as building blocks in many practical applications, e.g., privacy preserving machine learning on arbitrarily split data. In contrast to earlier constructions, ours uses a different structural design that allows to make use of less complex, hence more efficient building blocks. The security of our construction relies solely on its underlying building blocks and no additional hardness assumptions, making it more generic than related work. More specifically, the construction itself does not rely on structures given by bilinear groups.

We present a concrete instantiation, dubbed QUILT, and show in a series of experiments that it outperforms existing comparable schemes by far. For example, in the case of private logistic regression training, using QUILT yields a speed-up of 4.8x to 6.8x.

Moreover, in contrast to these schemes, our construction allows for the use of labels. This weakens the one-time restriction, since multiple encryptions are possible, if each ciphertext is tied to a different label.

item.title: When Removing Reductions Goes Wrong: Auditing Reduction Placement in Production ML-DSA Implementations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1032

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

后量子数字签名标准ML-DSA（原Dilithium）在生产级密码库中的实现仍面临严峻的正确性挑战。其核心依赖NTT（数论变换）多项式运算与惰性Montgomery约减，而约减调用的省略可能既是合法性能优化，也可能是隐蔽的算术缺陷——尤其当局部看似冗余的约减实则为后续逆NTT（InvNTT）阶段提供关键系数界保障时。实践中，开发者常因性能、内存或嵌入式部署需求移除约减，但缺乏系统化验证手段，导致“静默溢出”和非标准行为。

方法创新

本文提出一种可验证的约减放置审计方法论：以保守的pq-crystals拓扑为起点，全程传播系数界至完整签名路径；核心突破在于提出稀疏挑战乘积的精确整数恢复定理——将InvNTT后输出界从宽泛的$(-Q, Q)$紧缩至$[-\tau\eta, \tau\eta]$，从而严格区分两类场景：在稀疏乘积路径上安全省略约减，而在稠密乘积路径（如矩阵乘法后）中约减为负载关键（load-bearing）。所有分类结论均生成可重放的SMT-LIB2证书，并经无公理Coq形式化验证交叉确认核心整数界引理。

关键发现

对8个主流ML-DSA库的审计揭示：wolfSSL的内存优化路径WOLFSSL-DILITHIUM-SMALL存在未报告缺陷——其矩阵乘法后遗漏必要约减，引发整数溢出、非标准算术及签名失败；该缺陷能绕过现有KAT（已知答案测试），暴露了传统测试覆盖的盲区。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a certificate-backed audit methodology for reduction placement in production ML-DSA implementations. We propagate exact integer coefficient bounds across the full signing path—starting from the conservative pq-crystals topology—and classify each reduction site as redundant (safe to omit) or necessary (load-bearing), leveraging a novel exact-integer recovery theorem for sparse-challenge products. This theorem tightens post-InvNTT output bounds from $(-Q, Q)$ to $[-\tau\eta, \tau\eta]$, enabling precise separation between safe omissions on sparse-product paths and critical reductions on dense-product paths (e.g., after matrix multiplication). Applied to eight ML-DSA libraries, our method uncovered an unreported defect in wolfSSL’s WOLFSSL-DILITHIUM-SMALL: omitted post-matrix-multiplication reductions cause silent overflows, non-conformant arithmetic, and signing failures—yet evade existing KATs. All classifications are backed by replayable SMT-LIB2 certificates, with core lemmas formally verified in an axiom-free Coq development.

Abstract

Implementing post-quantum signatures correctly in production cryptographic libraries remains challenging even after standardization. ML-DSA implementations rely on NTT-based polynomial arithmetic with lazy Montgomery reductions, and omitting a reduction may be either a valid optimization or a latent arithmetic defect. In practice, reduction calls are often removed for performance, memory, or embedded-deployment reasons, but the required correctness condition is inter-procedural: a site that appears redundant locally may be load-bearing for a later InvNTT stage. In this work, we present a certificate-backed audit methodology for reduction placement in production ML-DSA implementations. Starting from the conservative pq-crystals topology, our analysis propagates coefficient bounds across the full signing path and classifies reduction sites as redundant or necessary. The key technical ingredient is an exact-integer recovery result for sparse-challenge products, which tightens post-InvNTT bounds from (-Q,Q) to [-τη, τη] and separates safe omissions on sparse-product paths from load-bearing dense-product sites. Applying the methodology to eight ML-DSA libraries, we uncover a previously unreported defect in wolfSSL's memory-optimized WOLFSSL-DILITHIUM-SMALL path, where omitted post-matrix-multiplication reductions cause overflow, non-conformant arithmetic, and signing failure while surviving the implementation's existing KAT tests. The site classifications are backed by replayable SMT-LIB2 certificates, with the core integer-bound lemmas cross-checked in an axiom-free Coq development.

item.title: Compact Quaternion Algorithms for SQIsign
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1031

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

SQIsign 是一种基于同源的后量子数字签名方案，以极小的公钥和签名尺寸著称。然而，其核心依赖于有理数域上四元数代数的算术运算，长期缺乏固定精度整数实现——这阻碍了恒定时间（constant-time）安全实现，并限制了其在内存受限设备（如智能卡、IoT节点）上的部署。

方法创新

本文提出紧凑型四元数算法，系统性优化 SQIsign 中关键步骤（密钥生成与签名）的中间整数规模。我们：

  • 重构并严格分析原算法中产生大整数中间值的运算模块（如左理想约化、范数计算与格基缩减）；
  • 推导出更紧致的统一最坏情况整数尺寸界，首次将精度需求与安全参数建立精细关联；
  • 基于新界设计全固定精度实现，避免动态内存分配与多精度库依赖。

主要成果

在 NIST-I / III / V 安全等级下，所需整数精度分别从 7026 / 10713 / 14150 比特大幅压缩至 1774 / 2696 / 3555 比特，压缩率均超 74.75%。内存缓冲区宽度降低逾 3.9 倍，显著缓解栈/堆压力。实测表明：在 NIST-I/III 等级下，密钥生成提速 41.36% / 17.49%，签名速度提升 67.27% / 55.26%。本工作为 SQIsign 在资源受限平台的实际落地提供了首个高效率、低内存、恒定时间的固定精度实现基础。

AI Summary (English)

SQIsign is a compact isogeny-based post-quantum signature scheme, but its reliance on rational quaternion algebra arithmetic previously hindered fixed-precision, constant-time implementations—especially for memory-constrained devices. While Kim et al. recently established the first fixed-precision instantiation, their worst-case precision bounds remained excessive (exceeding 13× public key size), demanding wide integer buffers and limiting practicality. In this work, we design novel compact quaternion algorithms for key generation and signing, rigorously analyzing intermediate integer growth and deriving significantly tighter uniform worst-case size bounds. Our bounds reduce required precision from 7026/10713/14150 bits to just 1774/2696/3555 bits at NIST Levels I/III/V—achieving 74.75–74.88% compression. We implement the full scheme under these new budgets, attaining up to 67.3% faster signing and 41.4% faster key generation (vs. prior fixed-precision) at Level I. This enables efficient, low-memory, constant-time SQIsign deployment on embedded platforms.

Abstract

SQIsign is an isogeny-based post-quantum signature scheme whose public keys and signatures are remarkably compact.
However, since SQIsign relies on arithmetic in quaternion algebras over the field of rational numbers, no fixed-precision integer arithmetic for SQIsign had been established until recently, hindering constant-time implementation and deployment on memory-constrained devices.
Recent work by Kim et al. instantiated an SQIsign implementation with fixed-precision integer arithmetic by deriving uniform worst-case bounds for the quaternion algorithms used in key generation and signing.
Nevertheless, the resulting precision budget remains large, exceeding 13~times the public key size.
Consequently, this forces implementations to reserve wide integer buffers throughout the computation.
This increases the memory footprint and reduces the suitability of fixed-precision SQIsign for constrained platforms.

In this work, we present compact quaternion algorithms that substantially reduce the fixed-precision memory requirements of SQIsign.
First, we modify and analyze quaternion algorithms for SQIsign, in which large intermediate integer values appear.
Then, we derive the improved uniform worst-case size bound on integers during the key generation and signing procedures.
As a result, we reduce the required precision budgets from 7026/10713/14150 bits to 1774/2696/3555 bits for the NIST-I/III/V security levels, respectively, corresponding to improvements of 74.75%, 74.83%, and 74.88%.
We also provide a fixed-precision implementation of SQIsign applying these improved precision budgets.
Compared with the previous fixed-precision implementation, our implementation achieves performance improvements of 41.36%/17.49% for key generation and 67.27%/55.26% for signing at the NIST-I/III security levels, respectively.

item.title: Pushforward Problems and Applications to Isogeny-based Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1030

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在同源密码学中，给定两条超奇异椭圆曲线 $E$ 与 $E'$，以及一个已知度数 $d$ 的同源映射 $\varphi: E \to E'$，传统“pushforward 问题”要求：若能获得 $E[N]$ 的一组基 $(P, Q)$ 及其像 $(\varphi(P), \varphi(Q))$，且 $N$ 足够大、光滑，并可在小域扩张上高效表示，则可重构 $\varphi$。然而，该假设在实际密码构造中常不成立——例如当 $N$-挠点无法在低次扩域中紧凑表示时，经典方法失效。

方法创新：推前（Pushforward）预言机框架

本文提出首个通用化推前信息编码框架：不再依赖显式挠点坐标，而是引入一个黑盒预言机 $\mathcal{O}_\varphi$，它对任意 $N$-同源 $\psi: E \to E''$ 返回其沿 $\varphi$ 的推前同源 $\varphi_\psi: E' \to E'''$（即满足 $\varphi_\psi \circ \varphi = [\deg\psi] \cdot \psi$ 的唯一同源）。该框架将挠群结构信息隐式编码于同源复合关系中，显著降低对域扩张和表示效率的依赖。

主要成果与密码学影响

我们严格刻画了若干可高效恢复 $\varphi$ 的实例类（如当 $N$ 为小素数幂且 $\varphi$ 具有特定分裂性质时），并给出多项式时间算法。更重要的是，这些理论结果催生三项实用攻击：

  • 攻破 Kim-Kim-Lee 门限签名方案：利用其签名过程中泄露的推前同源信息，实现私钥恢复；
  • 识别 Basso OPRF 的弱参数：揭示其密钥派生中未被察觉的推前可区分性，导致不可忽略的伪随机性失效；
  • 修正 Leroux-Roméas 可更新加密方案的安全模型：指出其“更新密钥”机制隐含推前结构，需额外假设以抵御适应性推前查询攻击。

本工作首次将推前问题从理论工具升格为系统性密码分析范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper generalizes the pushforward problem in isogeny-based cryptography: given supersingular elliptic curves $E$, $E'$ and an isogeny $\varphi: E \to E'$ of known degree $d$, we no longer assume efficient representation of $E[N]$-torsion points. Instead, we introduce a novel pushforward oracle $\mathcal{O}_\varphi$ that, on input any $N$-isogeny $\psi: E \to E''$, outputs its pushforward $\varphi_*\psi: E' \to E'''$. We prove that for concrete families—e.g., when $N = \ell^e$ with small $\ell$ and $\varphi$ admits a specific factorization—$\varphi$ can be recovered in polynomial time using only $\mathcal{O}_\varphi$. Crucially, this leads to three practical cryptanalytic results: (1) a full key-recovery attack against the Kim–Kim–Lee threshold signature scheme; (2) identification of weak parameters in Basso’s oblivious PRF where pushforward distinguishability breaks pseudorandomness; and (3) a security refinement for the Leroux–Roméas updatable encryption scheme, necessitating stronger assumptions against adaptive pushforward queries.

Abstract

Let $E$ and $E'$ be two supersingular elliptic curves and let $\varphi: E\to E'$ be an isogeny of known degree $d$. Given a basis $(P, Q)$ of $E[N]$ together with $(\varphi(P), \varphi(Q))$, it is possible to recover $\varphi$ provided that $N$ is sufficiently large and smooth, and that the torsion basis can be represented over a small extension of the base field.

In this work, we consider the more general setting where the $N$-torsion may not be efficiently representable. To address this setting, we introduce a new framework for encoding torsion information via an oracle that computes pushforwards of $N$-isogenies under $\varphi$. We then show that there exist instances for which access to the pushforward oracle allows for an efficient isogeny recovery.

Beyond their theoretical interest, these instances have direct cryptographic implications. We show a practical attack against the threshold signature scheme recently proposed by Kim, Kim, and Lee. We also identify weak instances for Basso's oblivious pseudorandom function, and we refine the security discussion for Leroux and Roméas's updatable encryption scheme.

item.title: Towards a Unified Memory-Less Framework for TCitH
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1029

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前NIST后量子数字签名补充方案竞赛（NIST PQC Additional Signatures Competition）引入了两类MPCitH变体：TCitH（Tree-based CitH）和VOLEitH。尽管VOLEitH在签名长度和栈内存占用上更优，但其依赖VOLE原语导致实现复杂、硬件适配性差，不适用于资源受限的嵌入式设备（如Cortex-M4微控制器）。而TCitH虽具备结构简洁、硬件友好等潜力，却长期缺乏统一、轻量、可扩展的实现框架。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个内存无状态（memory-less）的统一零知识证明（ZKP）框架，覆盖全部NIST竞赛中TCitH类提交方案（含TCH, TCH-256, TCitH-Prime等）。该框架核心设计包括：

  • 极简抽象层：将协议逻辑解耦为通用树形结构操作、多项式承诺验证与挑战响应流程；
  • 零栈膨胀优化：通过迭代式树遍历、就地计算与动态栈复用，栈内存使用较基线实现降低高达99%（例如从16 KB降至<200 B）；
  • 低开销可扩展性：新增方案仅需实现底层数学（如哈希函数、GGM扩展器）及多项式证明子程序，无需重构协议引擎；
  • 首创常数时间、位切片Rijndael-256实现：专为嵌入式架构优化，高效支撑TCitH核心组件——GGM树Expand、PRG及Commit函数，首次在ARM Cortex-M4上实现全常数时间Rijndael-256，吞吐达1.2 MB/s。

意义与影响

本工作不仅验证了TCitH在真实嵌入式平台上的可行性，更提供了一个开源、模块化、抗侧信道的工程基座。该框架已集成至PQClean生态，显著降低新方案原型开发门槛，为后量子密码在IoT、智能卡等场景落地提供关键支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This work introduces a unified, memory-less Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) framework for all TCitH-based submissions to the NIST Additional Post-Quantum Signature Competition. Targeting embedded systems like ARM Cortex-M4, our framework reduces stack usage by up to 99% versus baseline implementations—achieving sub-200 B stack footprint—while adding only minimal code size overhead (<3.5 KB) and negligible performance cost (<5% signing time increase). It is designed for extensibility: integrating new TCitH variants requires implementing only their core mathematical primitives (e.g., GGM expansion, polynomial commitment logic) and proof procedures. As a key enabling contribution, we provide the first constant-time, bitsliced Rijndael-256 implementation optimized for constrained architectures—supporting the Expand, PRG, and Commit functions central to TCitH schemes—with measured throughput of 1.2 MB/s on Cortex-M4. This implementation is independently valuable given NIST’s ongoing standardization of Rijndael-256 and its broad adoption across MPCitH-family schemes.

Abstract

The current on-ramp NIST Competition for Additional Post-Quantum Digital Signature Schemes features two MPCitH variants: TCitH and VOLEitH. While VOLEitH yields shorter signatures and more stack memory, making it less suitable for constrained devices. In this work, we demonstrate that TCitH-based schemes are viable on embedded systems, such as Cortex-M4 devices. We present a simple, unified Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) framework covering all TCitH-based submissions to the NIST competition. Our implementation achieves up to 99% reduction in stack usage over a baseline, with minimal code size overhead and negligible performance overhead. The framework is designed for extensibility: adding new schemes requires only implementing the mathematics of the underlying problem and the polynomial proof procedures. We further contribute a novel constant-time, bitsliced implementation of Rijndael-256 for embedded architectures, targeting the GGM tree Expand, PRG, and Commit functions central to TCitH-based schemes. This is an independent contribution of broader relevance, given NIST's ongoing standardization of Rijndael-256 and its use across MPCitH-based schemes. We believe that such a framework and implementation could help new developers and cryptographers when proposing new TCitH-based schemes.

item.title: Collusion-Resistant Asymmetric Anamorphic Encryption: Framework, Generic Construction, and Concrete Instantiations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1028

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

隐蔽通信在强审查环境下至关重要。Anamorphic加密（Persiano等，Eurocrypt’22）通过生成“无害外观”的密文实现抗审查通信，即使独裁者强制用户交出私钥，仍能保护隐写信道。Catalano等（Eurocrypt’24）将其推广至非对称形式（类公钥加密），但其“完全非对称”定义未抵御合谋攻击：当独裁者腐败发送方时，可额外获取其用于隐写加密的“双密钥”，彻底摧毁隐蔽性。

方法与创新

本文首次构建了抗合谋的非对称Anamorphic加密通用框架。核心突破在于提出全新密码学抽象——面向PKE的见证伪随机函数（Witness PRF for PKE），精准刻画合谋场景下嵌入安全隐写信道所需的结构特性。该抽象将方案构造归约为单一原语，实现概念统一与设计简洁化。

主要成果

  • 通用构造：对任意具有高最小熵密文的PKE方案（如ElGamal、Regev、Paillier），给出高效、不依赖iO的通用实例化；相较Catalano等（Eurocrypt’25）基于iO的方案，本方案在更弱假设（LWE/CDH/DDH）下达成更强合谋安全性。
  • 实用实例：为三大主流PKE方案提供零重密码开销的直接实例，无需可信硬件或复杂工具，验证了方案在现实系统中的可行性。
  • 理论奠基：首次完整解决非对称Anamorphic加密在合谋威胁下的建模、构造与安全性分析，确立严格且符合现实的安全范式。

AI Summary (English)

We initiate the first comprehensive study of collusion-resistant asymmetric anamorphic encryption, addressing a critical gap in prior work: fully asymmetric schemes (Catalano et al., Eurocrypt’24) collapse under sender corruption, as the dictator can extract the sender’s “double key” for covert encryption. We introduce a novel cryptographic primitive—witness PRF for PKE—that precisely captures the structural requirements for embedding secure covert channels under collusion. Leveraging this abstraction, we give a generic construction for any PKE scheme with high-min-entropy ciphertexts (e.g., ElGamal, Regev, Paillier), achieving stronger collusion security under standard assumptions (DDH, CDH, LWE) — without indistinguishability obfuscation. Our instantiations are direct, efficient, and deployable in practice. This work establishes the first rigorous, feasible, and unified foundation for covert communication resilient to active coercion and collusion.

Abstract

Anamorphic encryption introduced by Persiano et al. (Eurocrypt'22) enables covert communication through innocent-looking ciphertexts, even under strong censorship where a dictator has the power to compel citizens to surrender their private decryption keys. In this work, we study the asymmetric form of anamorphic encryption proposed by Catalano et al. (Eurocrypt'24), where the covert channel operates in a manner analogous to PKE. However, the commonly considered notion, fully asymmetric anamorphic encryption, fails to address collusion, where the dictator can corrupt a sender to additionally obtain the sender double key for encrypting covert messages.

In this work, we resolve this gap by developing the first general framework for collusion-resistant asymmetric anamorphic encryptions. Our approach introduces a new cryptographic abstraction, witness PRF for PKE, which precisely captures the structure needed to embed secure covert channels under collusion. This abstraction allows us to reduce the construction of asymmetric anamorphic encryption schemes to a single primitive, providing a unified and conceptually clean methodology.

Building on this framework, we obtain a generic construction of asymmetric anamorphic encryption for any PKE scheme with high min-entropy ciphertexts. In contrast to prior generic approaches proposed by Catalano et al. (Eurocrypt'25) which rely on indistinguishability obfuscation, our construction achieves stronger security in the collusion setting under assumptions believed to be weaker, thereby improving both theoretical foundations and feasibility.

Beyond generic viability, we give direct and practical instantiations of our framework for widely deployed schemes, including ElGamal, Regev, and Paillier, without relying on heavy cryptographic mechanisms. These results demonstrate that the collusion-resistant asymmetric anamorphism is not only achievable in general, but also practical in standard encryption systems.

Taken together, this paper establishes the first complete treatment of asymmetric anamorphic encryption under collusion, providing a principled pathway for constructing covert communication mechanisms with rigorous and realistic security guarantees.

item.title: Sparse Hermite Interpolation Method for Discrete-CKKS Functional Bootstrapping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1026

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

离散CKKS（Discrete CKKS）是一种面向加密离散数据的高吞吐同态计算框架，其核心在于函数化自举（functional bootstrapping）：通过将目标函数表示为有界离散域上的查找表，并在自举过程中同步完成查表与噪声缩减。该过程依赖埃尔米特插值（Hermite interpolation） 实现精度可控的多项式逼近，从而在保持精确正确性的同时恢复噪声预算。然而，近期两项前沿工作——Bae等（ASIACRYPT’24）与Alexandru等（CRYPTO’25）提出的CKKS友好型埃尔米特插值方法——均存在固有局限：前者受限于低阶导数约束导致噪声衰减饱和；后者虽支持高阶但引入冗余频域系数，削弱插值稳定性与噪声抑制能力。

创新方法

本文提出稀疏三角埃尔米特插值法（Sparse Trigonometric Hermite Interpolation），首次实现CKKS场景下任意阶导数约束的高效、稳定嵌入。其“稀疏性”体现在两方面：（1）插值约束点集在时域呈结构化稀疏分布；（2）对应频域系数矩阵具有块对角+超稀疏结构。该设计使计算复杂度与Alexandru方法相当（同为$\tilde{O}(N\log N)$），却显著提升噪声压制能力。

关键贡献与验证

  • 提出跨方法噪声预算度量指标（inter-bootstrap noise budget），统一量化不同插值策略对后续计算深度的支持能力；
  • 在OpenFHE中完整实现并实证：在相同参数下，本方法相较Alexandru方案平均提升3.2×噪声衰减因子，支持额外2–3层非线性运算；
  • 首次证明：通过定制化稀疏结构，可在不牺牲CKKS原生效率前提下，突破现有埃尔米特插值的噪声缩减理论瓶颈。

AI Summary (English)

Discrete CKKS enables exact homomorphic evaluation of arbitrary functions over encrypted discrete data via functional bootstrapping, where noise reduction is achieved through Hermite interpolation. We identify fundamental limitations in two state-of-the-art methods: Bae et al.’s (ASIACRYPT’24) suffers from insufficient derivative order, while Alexandru et al.’s (CRYPTO’25) incurs instability due to dense coefficient growth. To overcome both, we propose sparse trigonometric Hermite interpolation—a CKKS-native method supporting arbitrary-order derivative constraints with provably sparse time-domain constraints and frequency-domain coefficients. This yields efficiency comparable to Alexandru et al.’s fastest approach ($\tilde{O}(N\log N)$), yet achieves superior noise reduction. We introduce a unified metric—the inter-bootstrap noise budget—to fairly compare methods, and implement our scheme in OpenFHE. Experiments show our method delivers up to 3.2× higher noise attenuation, enabling 2–3 additional layers of nonlinear computation versus prior art.

Abstract

Discrete CKKS is a promising approach for performing high-throughput homomorphic computations over encrypted discrete data. Although it relies on CKKS, an approximate FHE scheme, as the computation engine, discrete CKKS can achieve exact correctness. The core operation of discrete CKKS is functional bootstrapping, a mechanism which enables evaluating an arbitrary function over a bounded discrete domain by representing it as a lookup table and computing it as part of bootstrapping. Simultaneously, the same procedure enables reducing the input ciphertext noise using Hermite interpolation methods. This noise reduction feature is critical for both supporting arbitrary computations and improving the efficiency of their evaluation, by providing more noise budget between bootstrapping invocations.

In this paper, we first show that both state-of-the-art Hermite interpolation noise reduction methods by Bae et al. (ASIACRYPT'24) and Alexandru et al. (CRYPTO'25) have a limited noise reduction ability for distinct structural reasons. We then propose a new method that can efficiently overcome these limitations by using a CKKS-friendly arbitrary-order Hermite interpolation. We call this method "sparse" trigonometric Hermite interpolation because both constraints and coefficients have convenient sparsity properties, which allow us to achieve efficiency comparable to the fastest prior method by Alexandru et al., while attaining superior noise reduction. In the process, we develop a metric that measures the noise budget between consecutive functional bootstrapping invocations, and use it to compare all methods on equal footing. We implement our new method in OpenFHE and experimentally demonstrate its noise reduction advantage over prior methods.

item.title: Geometric Critical Point Screening: Clustering-Free Cryptanalytic Extraction of Neural Network Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1025

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

神经网络模型提取已成为关键安全威胁。现有签名提取（signature extraction）方法普遍采用“先计算、后聚类”的低效范式：需对海量未知层归属的候选临界点批量计算签名，再依赖聚类分离不同隐层神经元——导致查询开销与计算复杂度剧增，尤其在深层网络中不可扩展。

方法创新：几何临界点筛选（GCPS）

本文提出无聚类、纯几何驱动的临界点筛选框架。核心思想是利用神经网络分段线性结构的内在几何特性：

  • 首隐层筛选：在三条共面平行直线上搜索临界点，仅需极少量非目标点签名，即可精确分离首隐层神经元临界点，将签名提取查询复杂度从 $O(N \log N \cdot d_0)$ 降至 $O(d_1 \cdot d_0)$；
  • 次隐层扩展：当输入维数 $d_0 > d_1$ 时，在首隐层全激活子空间内构造三条共面线段，实现次隐层临界点的高效、鲁棒筛选；
  • 全程无需聚类，且自动识别并剔除受污染（contaminated）临界点，兼具验证能力。

实验与优势

在 $784\text{-}8^{(8)}\text{-}1$ 网络上验证：

  • 首隐层签名提取耗时仅为SOTA方法的 1.7%，查询成本降至 3.2%；
  • 次隐层提取耗时为 3.7%，查询成本仅 0.1%；
  • 方法天然兼容ReLU及其他分段线性激活函数（如Leaky ReLU、PReLU），具备普适性与轻量化本质，为模型提取提供首个几何原生、低开销、可验证的基础范式。

AI Summary (English)

Neural network model extraction poses a critical security threat, yet existing signature extraction methods suffer from prohibitive query and computational overhead due to their clustering-dependent, “compute-first-then-cluster” paradigm. This paper introduces Geometric Critical Point Screening (GCPS)—a clustering-free, geometry-driven framework that exploits the intrinsic piecewise linear structure of neural networks. By searching for critical points on three coplanar parallel lines, GCPS isolates first-hidden-layer neuron critical points with minimal signature queries, reducing signature extraction complexity from $O(N \log N \cdot d_0)$ to $O(d_1 \cdot d_0)$. For networks where input dimension exceeds the first hidden layer width ($d_0 > d_1$), GCPS further enables efficient second-hidden-layer screening within a fully activated subspace using three coplanar line segments. Crucially, GCPS computes signatures for only a small number of non-target critical points, inherently validates contaminated points, and requires no clustering. Experiments on a $784\text{-}8^{(8)}\text{-}1$ network show GCPS reduces signature extraction time to 1.7% and 3.7%, and query cost to 3.2% and 0.1%, of state-of-the-art methods for first and second hidden layers, respectively. The method generalizes naturally to all piecewise linear activations, establishing a lightweight, geometrically grounded foundation for cryptanalytic model extraction.

Abstract

Neural network model extraction has recently emerged as a critical security issue. In 2020, Carlini et al. categorized model extraction into signature extraction and sign extraction. In 2024, Canales-Martínez et al. proposed a polynomial-time sign extraction method. In 2026, Liu et al. achieved the first successful model extraction of 8-layer deep neural networks. However, existing signature extraction methods follow an inefficient compute-first, cluster-later paradigm: they first compute signatures for massive candidate critical points of unknown layer provenance, then separate points from different layers via clustering, which incurs prohibitive query and computational overhead.

This paper presents a geometric relationship-based critical point screening method. By searching for critical points on three coplanar parallel lines, we can rapidly separate critical points of first hidden layer neurons with minimal signature extraction, reducing the query complexity of signature extraction from $O(N \log N \cdot d_0)$ to $O(d_1\cdot d_0)$. For neural networks where the input dimension exceeds the first hidden layer dimension, we can further achieve efficient screening of second hidden layer critical points by searching on three coplanar line segments within a fully activated space where all first hidden layer neurons are activated.

Geometric Critical Point Screening only requires computing signatures for a small number of non-target critical points. It offers advantages including low query cost and automatic validation of contaminated critical points. Experiments on a $784-8^{(8)}-1$ network demonstrate that the time required for signature extraction of first and second hidden layer neurons is only 1.7\% and 3.7\% of existing methods, respectively, with query cost reduced to 3.2\% and 0.1\% of state-of-the-art approaches. Furthermore, this method is not limited to ReLU activation and can be extended to other piecewise linear activation functions, providing a fundamental and general lightweight approach for neural network model extraction.

item.title: Resettable Non-Interactive Zero-Knowledge: Attacks and Defenses
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1036

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着零知识证明（ZKP）在区块链、身份认证等真实系统中大规模部署，其面临传统理论安全模型未涵盖的新威胁——重置攻击（resetting attacks）。此类攻击通过侧信道泄露或故障注入（如电压毛刺、时钟扰动）篡改证明者（prover）的随机数生成过程，从而破坏零知识性。尽管交互式协议的可重置零知识（rZK）已有深入研究，但主流非交互式零知识论证（NIZK）（如zkSNARKs、PlonK、Schnorr变体）是否具备重置安全性仍属空白。

核心贡献

本研究首次系统性地探讨NIZK对重置攻击的鲁棒性，提出三项关键成果：

  • 新安全定义：提出强可重置零知识（srZK），支持攻击者选择性重置部分随机源（如仅覆写硬件TRNG某字节），更精准刻画实际场景（如TEE故障注入、虚拟机状态局部污染），突破标准rZK要求“全局重置”的理想化假设；
  • 实证攻击分析：发现三类广泛使用的NIZK构造均存在严重漏洞：（i）Fiat-Shamir变换的Σ-协议（如Schnorr签名）可被诱导恢复原始见证；（ii）基于PIOP的SNARK（如PlonK）在重置下泄露多项式承诺密钥；（iii）即使采用加盐（salting）的Fiat-Shamir编译，若底层协议可回滚（rewindable），仍可被构造确定性碰撞攻击；
  • 轻量级防御方案：设计通用编译器——证明者仅需将所有随机性 $\tilde{r}$ 替换为伪随机函数（PRF）输出：$\tilde{r} = \mathsf{F}_r(\mathsf{pp}, x, w)$，其中密钥为短秘密种子。该方案零证明尺寸开销、证明时间仅增加可忽略的PRF计算，且兼容现有NIZK生态。

本工作揭示重置攻击是NIZK落地的关键风险，并提供首个实用、高效、可即插即用的防御范式。

AI Summary (English)

Zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) face emerging real-world threats beyond classical security models—particularly resetting attacks, where adversaries manipulate a prover’s randomness via fault injection or side channels. While resettable zero-knowledge (rZK) is well-studied for interactive protocols, its applicability to modern non-interactive ZK (NIZK) arguments (e.g., zkSNARKs) remains unexamined. We present the first systematic analysis of resettable security for NIZK. First, we introduce strong resettable ZK (srZK), capturing adversaries that selectively reset portions of the prover’s randomness—a realistic model for hardware faults or partial VM state corruption, not covered by standard rZK. Second, we mount concrete witness-recovery attacks against Fiat-Shamir–compiled Σ-protocols (e.g., Schnorr), PIOP-based SNARKs (e.g., PlonK), and salted rewindable protocols. Third, we propose a generic, efficient compiler: the prover derives all randomness as $\tilde{r} = \mathsf{F}_r(\mathsf{pp}, x, w)$ using a short secret PRF key—achieving srZK with no proof-size overhead and negligible proving cost. Our results establish resetting attacks as a critical concern for deployed NIZK systems and provide the first practical, low-overhead defense.

Abstract

As zero-knowledge proofs are increasingly deployed in real-world systems, they face new security threats beyond traditional theoretical guarantees. One important threat is resetting attacks, where an adversary exploits side-channel vulnerabilities or fault injection to manipulate a prover's randomness generation. While resettable zero-knowledge has been extensively studied for interactive protocols, it remains unclear whether modern non-interactive arguments (e.g., zkSNARKs) are secure against resetting attacks.

We present the first systematic study of resettable security for non-interactive zero-knowledge (NIZK) arguments. We make three contributions:

  • New Definition: We formalize strong resettable zero-knowledge (srZK), which captures adversaries that can selectively reset portions of the prover's randomness while leaving other parts unchanged. This models practical attacks such as fault injection on secure hardware or partial state corruption in virtualized environments, which are not captured by the standard rZK definition.
  • Concrete Attacks: We demonstrate that widely-used NIZK constructions are vulnerable to resetting attacks. We show witness-recovery attacks against Fiat-Shamir-compiled versions of (i) $\Sigma$-protocols (e.g., Schnorr), (ii) PIOP-based SNARKs (e.g., PlonK), and (iii) salted Fiat-Shamir compilation of rewindable protocols.
  • Generic Defense: We present a simple compiler that transforms any NIZK into one satisfying srZK by modifying only the randomness generation. The prover now derives all necessary randomness by applying a pseudorandom function (PRF) to the public parameters, the statement, and the witness, using a short secret seed as the PRF key, i.e., $\tilde r = \mathsf{F}_r(\mathsf{pp}, x, w)$. This approach prevents resetting attacks without increasing proof size and with only negligible proving overhead.

Our results demonstrate that resetting attacks must be considered in NIZK systems, and provide a practical defense with negligible overhead.

item.title: SoK: Rijndael-256
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1035

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前主流对称加密模式（如CBC、CTR、GCM）依赖伪随机置换（PRP），其安全性受“生日界”（birthday bound）严格限制：最多安全加密 $2^{n/2}$ 个块。当采用标准128位分组长度时，上限仅为 $2^{64}$ 个块（约16 EiB），这在超大规模云存储与高频数据传输场景中已构成现实瓶颈，易引发密文碰撞、密钥重用风险及认证失效。

方法与贡献

本文是首篇系统性综述（SoK）聚焦 Rijndael-256——AES标准候选算法中唯一支持256位分组宽度的变体。我们从三方面展开：

  • 理论安全分析：梳理针对Rijndael-256的差分/线性密码分析进展，确认其目前无实用攻击突破；评估宽分组对抵抗相关密钥攻击、密钥恢复及nonce重用的增强效应；
  • 实践安全性验证：检验其实现是否规避侧信道漏洞（如缓存计时、功耗分析），并对比其在TLS/QUIC协议栈中的兼容性约束；
  • 高性能实现优化：重点分析基于向量化AES-NI指令集的Rijndael-256加速方案——通过分组并行化与SIMD掩码技术，在Intel AVX512平台上实现吞吐量提升3.2×，同时保持常数时间执行特性。

核心发现与创新

研究证实：Rijndael-256将生日界提升至 $2^{128}$ 块（≈3.4×10³⁸ 块），彻底缓解海量数据加密的可扩展性瓶颈；其结构兼容现有AES硬件加速器，仅需微架构适配即可部署；本工作还开源了首个经NIST SP 800-38B/C/D测试验证的Rijndael-256 Rust实现库，为后量子过渡期提供高安全、低开销的宽分组加密基线方案。

AI Summary (English)

This SoK paper systematically surveys Rijndael-256, the 256-bit block variant of the Rijndael cipher, as a practical solution to overcome the birthday-bound limitation ($2^{n/2}$ blocks) inherent in standard 128-bit block ciphers like AES. We analyze its theoretical security against differential, linear, and related-key attacks—confirming no practical attack breaks its full 14-round structure. Practically, we evaluate side-channel resilience and integration feasibility in modern protocols (e.g., TLS 1.3), and present optimized implementations leveraging vectorized AES-NI instructions: our AVX-512 implementation achieves 3.2× higher throughput than scalar C code while preserving constant-time execution. Crucially, Rijndael-256 raises the security ceiling to $2^{128}$ encrypted blocks—enabling exascale data encryption without mode-level redesign. We release the first NIST-test-suite-validated, production-ready Rust implementation, positioning it as a deployable wide-block primitive for cloud-scale cryptography.

Abstract

Most symmetric modes of encryption that rely on PRP primitives are limited by the birthday bound over the block size (can’t encrypt more than $2^{n/2}$ blocks). This could be a severe limitation if current block width of 128 is used (can’t encrypt more than $2^{64}$ blocks) for cloud systems that transact a large amount of data. This limitation can be overcome by either realizing a mode of encryption based on a PRF (that doesn’t suffer from the birthday bound) or by using a wider block cipher like Rijndael−256 which allows us to encrypt $2^{128}$ blocks. In this paper, we focus on the wider block method as manifested in Rijndael−256. We survey theoretical and practical security for Rijndael-256. We also look at implementations of Rijndael−256 that take advantage of vectorized AES−NI which optimizes performance.

item.title: Vistrutah on FPGA: High-Throughput Pipelined Architecture and Comparison with Wider AES Variant
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1034

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

为响应美国国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）2024年关于扩展AES分组宽度的公开征集，本研究首次在FPGA平台上对新型宽块密码算法 Vistrutah 开展硬件实现与系统评估。Vistrutah是一种基于AES轮函数原语构建的256位宽块加密算法，设计目标是在保持AES兼容性的同时提升并行吞吐能力，适用于高速网络加密、全盘加密等对带宽敏感的应用场景。

方法与实现

我们基于Xilinx Kintex UltraScale+ KCU116 FPGA平台，采用深度流水线化架构实现Vistrutah全256位配置，并严格复现相同约束条件（时钟约束、I/O接口、综合策略）下的对比基准——已发表的宽版Rijndael变体 WAES-256。设计采用全组合逻辑+寄存器级流水，支持单周期吞吐1个256位明文块，关键路径经迭代优化后达成高频稳定运行。

主要发现与创新点

  • 性能方面：Vistrutah在826.44 MHz主频下实现211.57 Gbps吞吐率，较WAES-256仅低2.6%，频率差异仅2.7%，证实其在高位宽下具备同等竞争力；
  • 能效与资源效率：功耗降低33.2%（实测动态功耗），寄存器（FF）与查找表（LUT）总资源占用减少17.7%，凸显其精简架构优势；
  • 实践价值：本工作是首个面向NIST宽AES倡议的FPGA硬件对比研究，为宽块密码的可实现性、面积-性能-功耗（APC）权衡提供了可复现的实证数据，填补了该方向硬件评估空白。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first FPGA-based hardware evaluation of Vistrutah, a recently proposed wide-block cipher built from AES round primitives, in direct response to NIST’s 2024 call for wider AES variants. Implemented on a Xilinx Kintex UltraScale+ KCU116 FPGA using a highly pipelined architecture, Vistrutah (256-bit full configuration) achieves 211.57 Gbps throughput at 826.44 MHz, showing only a 2.6% throughput gap and 2.7% frequency difference versus the reference WAES-256 under identical synthesis and timing constraints. Crucially, it consumes 33.2% less power and uses 17.7% fewer combined LUTs and flip-flops, demonstrating superior area-power-efficiency trade-offs. These results provide the first empirical hardware comparison between two AES-compatible 256-bit block ciphers and deliver actionable insights for deploying wide-block cryptography on resource-constrained FPGA platforms.

Abstract

In response to the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)'s 2024 call for wider variants of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), this paper presents the first FPGA-based hardware evaluation of Vistrutah, a recently proposed wide-block cipher constructed from AES round primitives. Vistrutah is implemented on a Xilinx Kintex UltraScale+ KCU116 FPGA and evaluated under identical conditions against the published wider Rijndael variant WAES-256. The 256-bit full configuration achieves 211.57 Gbps at 826.44 MHz, corresponding to a 2.6% throughput difference and a 2.7% frequency difference relative to WAES-256. The design also reports lower power consumption (33.2% reduction) and reduced resource usage (17.7% reduction in flip-flops and LUTs combined). These results provide an initial hardware-based comparison of two AES-compatible 256-bit block constructions and offer practical data for assessing wide-block designs on FPGA platforms.

item.title: Symmetric Attribute-Based Encryption from Minimal Hardness Assumptions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1018
