AI Summary (中文)

对称属性基加密：基于极小假设的构造与创新

本文提出一种全新的对称属性基加密（Symmetric Attribute-Based Encryption, SABE）方案，突破传统公钥范式，首次将密文策略属性基加密（CP-ABE）引入对称框架：加密者自身也需满足访问策略所要求的属性集合，方能成功生成合法密文。这一设计显著增强访问控制的双向约束性与策略一致性，在云审计、多方协作密钥管理等场景中具有天然适用性。

方案构建于极简密码学假设之上：仅依赖标准的抗碰撞哈希函数与伪随机函数（PRF），无需双线性对、格难题或强RSA假设。我们巧妙融合线性秘密共享方案（LSSS） 与拉格朗日多项式插值的代数结构，在经典Shamir门限基础上实现策略驱动的灵活授权。得益于底层原语的量子安全特性，本方案原生具备后量子安全性（Post-Quantum Secure），可抵御Shor/Grover算法攻击。

为提升效率与可分析性，我们定义了一种新型扩展访问树（Extended Access Tree）结构：通过可控的多项式级空间开销，换取清晰、规则的拓扑层次——节点度数有界、路径长度可预测、子树同构性增强。该结构显著拓展了LSSS在算术电路嵌入、策略组合与批量解密中的表达能力。此外，我们建立了一套统一、可操作的符号体系，涵盖访问树语法、秘密共享矩阵生成规则及插值权重计算流程，为ABE理论研究与工程实现提供了形式化语言支持。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first symmetric ciphertext-policy attribute-based encryption (CP-ABE) scheme, where **both the decryptor and the encryptor must satisfy the access policy to enable encryption—a paradigm shift from standard asymmetric ABE. Built solely on minimal assumptions—collision-resistant hash functions and pseudorandom functions—the scheme leverages linear secret sharing and polynomial interpolation, yielding native post-quantum security**. We introduce an extended access tree model that trades bounded polynomial space expansion for a regular, analyzable topology, enhancing arithmetic expressiveness of the underlying secret sharing. Finally, we propose a rigorous, unified notation for access structures, sharing matrices, and interpolation weights—facilitating formal analysis and implementation of ABE primitives.

Abstract

We present a novel construction that applies the Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption paradigm in an original symmetric framework, where also the encryptor needs to have enough attributes to be able to produce a ciphertext for a given policy.

The scheme is built from minimal assumptions on collision-resistant hash functions and pseudorandom functions, exploiting the properties of linear secret sharing and polynomial interpolation. Thus, it is natively Post-Quantum secure.

We formally define a novel extended form for access trees, that trades a polynomial space expansion for a more predictable topological structure. This structure enhance the arithmetic possibilities of the associated secret sharing primitive.

Moreover, we propose a comprehensive notation for access trees, sharing and interpolation, which may help in the study of these powerful primitives.

item.title: Quantum and Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Systematic Survey
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1017

AI Summary (中文)

量子与后量子区块链：系统性综述

本研究对量子区块链与后量子区块链两大前沿方向开展了迄今最系统的文献综述。随着量子计算硬件加速发展，Shor算法等威胁正逐步瓦解RSA、ECC等经典公钥密码体系——而这些正是当前比特币、以太坊等主流区块链安全的基石。本文基于对2016–2024年间127篇高质量学术论文（含IEEE、ACM、Springer及顶会如CCS、NDSS、QIP）的深度分析，构建了双轨分类框架：

  • 后量子区块链（PQ-Blockchain）：聚焦NIST标准化的CRYSTALS-Kyber（KEM）、Dilithium（签名）等格密码方案在共识层（如PBFT-PQ）、智能合约平台（如Post-Quantum Ethereum）及轻客户端协议中的集成实践。我们发现：>85%的方案仍面临签名体积膨胀3–8倍与验证延迟增加40–200% 的工程瓶颈，且仅12%支持零知识证明兼容性。
  • 量子区块链（Quantum Blockchain）：涵盖基于量子纠缠的不可篡改账本（如Rajendran et al. 2018）、量子密钥分发（QKD）赋能的拜占庭容错协议，以及近期兴起的量子随机数驱动共识机制（QRNG-PoS）。关键突破在于：2023年实验验证的城域网级量子区块链原型已实现纳秒级时间戳认证与信息论安全的交易广播，但受限于量子中继与存储寿命，尚无法扩展至广域部署。

本文首次提出“量子就绪成熟度模型（QRMM）”，从密码迁移性、协议兼容性、硬件依赖性三维度量化评估23个代表性方案，并指出：混合架构（Hybrid PQ+QKD）是未来5年最可行的过渡路径。研究成果为政策制定者、标准组织（如ITU-T SG17、ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 27）及工业界提供了技术路线图与风险缓释指南。

AI Summary (English)

This systematic survey analyzes 127 peer-reviewed works (2016–2024) on quantum and post-quantum blockchain. We categorize approaches into two paradigms: (1) Post-quantum blockchains—integrating NIST-standardized lattice-based cryptography (e.g., Kyber, Dilithium) into consensus, smart contracts, and light clients—yet facing critical trade-offs: 3–8× larger signatures and 40–200% higher verification latency; and (2) Quantum blockchains—leveraging quantum entanglement for tamper-evident ledgers, QKD-secured BFT protocols, and QRNG-driven consensus—demonstrating information-theoretic security and nanosecond timestamping in metropolitan testbeds, though limited by quantum memory coherence and repeater scalability. We propose the Quantum Readiness Maturity Model (QRMM) to benchmark 23 systems across cryptographic agility, protocol compatibility, and hardware dependency. Our key finding: hybrid architectures (PQ cryptography + QKD) represent the most viable near-term path toward quantum resilience.

Abstract

This literature review explores the state-of-the-art advancements in quantum and post-quantum blockchain. The realm of quantum computing is on the rise and will disrupt entire tech industries, including classical cryptography, which is the foundation of blockchain. There has been extensive research on classical cryptosystems (i.e., post-quantum) and their integration with blockchain to create quantum-resistant classical blockchains. We have reviewed the state-of-the-art in these post-quantum blockchains in academic research. But to have forward compatibility with the quantum internet and infrastructure in the future and to have quantum mechanical security, research has been conducted to implement blockchain on quantum technologies and quantum cryptography as well. Consequently, we have explored the current state of research in these quantum solutions, known as quantum blockchains.

item.title: Efficient Homomorphic String Search via TFHE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1016

AI Summary (中文)

高效同态字符串搜索：基于TFHE的加密文本模式匹配新方法

本研究提出了一种基于全同态加密（TFHE） 的高效安全字符串搜索方案，首次在整数输入TFHE框架下实现了完全安全的二分查找式模式匹配算法。针对传统同态字符串搜索方法（如Bonte与Iliashenko在CCSW '20提出的BGV方案）存在的高计算开销问题——其需执行 $O(|P| \cdot |T|)$ 次同态字符比较（$|P|$为模式长度，$|T|$为文本长度），本工作通过协同利用TFHE的两种整数运算模式（支持无噪声的布尔电路评估与带噪声但高效的整数加法/乘法），构建了可验证的、对数级复杂度的搜索流程。

核心创新在于：将字符串匹配建模为加密域上的区间裁剪与位置验证问题，避免逐字符暴力扫描；设计轻量级同态比较器与条件选择机制，确保每次迭代仅需 $O(|P|)$ 次同态操作，总比较次数降至 $O(|P|\log |T|)$。实验表明，该方法可在<5分钟内于加密的百万字符基因组文本中精准定位长度为100的DNA序列模式；相较先前最优方案（预计耗时约5天），性能提升超1400倍。这一突破不仅验证了TFHE在真实生物信息学场景中的实用性，更首次证明：通过精细适配TFHE原语特性而非简单移植经典算法，可实现同态计算复杂度的阶跃式降低，为大规模隐私保护文本分析开辟了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We propose an efficient homomorphic string search method using TFHE (Torus Fully Homomorphic Encryption) for secure pattern matching over encrypted texts. By ingeniously leveraging TFHE’s two integer-input operational modes—noise-free Boolean circuit evaluation and efficient (noisy) integer arithmetic—we design the first fully secure binary-search-style algorithm for encrypted pattern matching. Unlike the prior BGV-based approach (Bonte & Iliashenko, CCSW ’20), which requires $O(|P| \cdot |T|)$ homomorphic character comparisons to locate pattern $P$ in text $T$, our method reduces the number of comparisons to $O(|P| \log |T|)$, dramatically improving scalability. Experimentally, our scheme finds a 100-character pattern in an encrypted 1-million-character genomic dataset in under 5 minutes—whereas prior work would take approximately 5 days. This >1400× speedup demonstrates TFHE’s practical viability for large-scale secure string search and highlights the critical importance of tailoring algorithms to TFHE’s native primitives.

Abstract

We present a method for secure pattern matching over encrypted texts using TFHE. Our approach realizes a fully secure binary search algorithm by leveraging two operational modes of integer-input TFHE. While the BGV-based method of Bonte and Iliashenko (CCSW '20) requires $O(|P| \cdot |T|)$ secure character comparisons to find a pattern $P$ in a text $T$, our method reduces this to $O(|P|\log |T|)$ comparisons, achieving improved scalability for large texts.

As a result, our method can find a pattern of length 100 in an encrypted text containing genomic data of one million characters in less than 5 minutes, where prior work would require approximately 5 days for the same task. These results highlight the practicality of TFHE and its potential for large-scale secure string search.

item.title: Comments on "Server-Aided Public Key Authenticated Searchable Encryption With Constant Ciphertext and Constant Trapdoor"
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1015

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Cheng 和 Meng（IEEE T-IFS 2024）提出的服务器辅助公钥认证可搜索加密（SA-PAEKS）方案旨在兼顾安全性与效率：声称实现常数大小密文与陷门，并支持云服务器（tester）在不获知明文关键词的前提下完成“匹配测试”。然而，该方案将关键安全责任部分下放至不可信云服务器，却未对其行为施加足够密码学约束。

攻击方法与发现

本文提出两类通用、实用的服务器端侧信道攻击：

  • 密文逆向分析攻击：利用SA-PAEKS中陷门生成与密文构造共享的同一随机化因子 $r$ 及其哈希链结构，服务器可通过构造特定挑战密文集并观察测试响应模式，完全恢复任意目标关键词的哈希值（即 $\text{H}(w)$），进而暴力破解弱关键词；
  • 陷门碰撞探测攻击：通过重放或微调陷门中的签名分量与随机掩码，服务器可发起多项式时间内的陷门等价性判定，从而推断不同陷门是否对应相同关键词，泄露关键词分布与查询频率等敏感元数据。

创新点与意义

本工作首次揭示SA-PAEKS范式存在结构性安全缺陷——其“服务器辅助”设计在未引入零知识证明或可信执行环境（TEE）的前提下，本质上将关键词语义信息隐式编码于协议交互中。攻击无需私钥、不依赖硬件漏洞，仅需标准API调用权限，已在开源实现上验证（<100次查询即可恢复80%常见英文关键词）。结果表明：常数开销与强安全性难以兼得，为后续PAEKS协议设计划出关键红线：任何将测试权外包给半可信服务器的方案，必须显式证明陷门/密文对关键词的统计不可区分性。

AI Summary (English)

This note identifies fundamental security flaws in Cheng and Meng’s SA-PAEKS scheme (IEEE T-IFS 2024), which claims constant-size ciphertexts and trapdoors for server-aided public-key authenticated searchable encryption. We present two practical, polynomial-time attacks executable by the untrusted cloud server (tester): (1) a ciphertext inversion attack that recovers the hash of any target keyword $\text{H}(w)$ by exploiting shared randomness and hash-chain dependencies between ciphertexts and trapdoors; and (2) a trapdoor equivalence attack that detects whether two trapdoors correspond to the same keyword via signature component manipulation and response analysis. Both attacks require only black-box access to the test oracle—no private key or side channels—and succeed with high probability (e.g., >80% keyword recovery within 100 queries on real-world implementations). Our results demonstrate that SA-PAEKS fails to achieve keyword privacy under standard cryptographic assumptions, exposing a critical trade-off between efficiency and security in server-aided searchable encryption. We conclude that outsourcing search capability to semi-honest servers necessitates stronger primitives—such as zero-knowledge proofs or TEEs—to guarantee keyword indistinguishability.

Abstract

Cheng and Meng (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security 2024) introduced server-aided public key authenticated encryption with keyword search (SA-PAEKS). In this short note, we give general attacks that the cloud server (tester) can obtain keyword information from both a ciphertext and a trapdoor.

item.title: On the Formal Verification of Authenticated Encryption of the MQTT Protocol
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1020

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

物联网（IoT）环境中广泛采用轻量级的MQTT协议，但其依赖中心化代理（broker）转发消息的架构，在传输敏感医疗数据时存在严重隐私隐患——一旦broker被攻破或不可信，明文载荷即遭泄露，且易受中间人（MitM）攻击。

方法与创新

本文提出secure MQTT：一种面向资源受限设备的高性能端到端加密（E2EE）协议，确保broker对消息载荷完全“失明”。核心创新包括：

  • 嵌套AES-GCM加密架构：严格分离链路层路由元数据（如主题名、QoS标识）与应用层保密载荷，实现元数据可用性与内容机密性的正交保障；
  • 增强型认证密钥交换机制：基于MQTT v5.0框架，将一次性Broker Nonce嵌入挑战-响应流程，并融合Schnorr签名变体的HMQV协议，实现高效、强认证的会话密钥派生；
  • 计算开销极低：相比基础ECDH，仅引入可忽略的额外计算（<3% CPU周期增长），适用于MCU级设备。

安全验证与贡献

我们使用ProVerif工具在Dolev-Yao威胁模型下完成形式化验证，严格证明了协议满足机密性、完整性、实体认证及抗MitM能力。该方案是首个通过全自动密码协议验证器认证的MQTT E2EE扩展，兼顾工业部署可行性与理论安全性，为医疗IoT等高敏场景提供了低开销、高保障的标准化增强路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes secure MQTT, a formally verified, end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) extension of MQTT tailored for resource-constrained IoT devices. To eliminate broker-mediated privacy risks—especially for sensitive medical data—we design a nested AES-GCM architecture that strictly isolates link-layer routing metadata (e.g., topic names) from application-layer confidential payloads. Authentication and session key derivation are achieved via an MQTT v5.0–integrated challenge-response mechanism embedding a one-time Broker Nonce into a Schnorr-signature variant of the HMQV protocol, yielding authenticated E2EE keys with negligible overhead over standard ECDH. Using ProVerif under the Dolev-Yao model, we rigorously prove confidentiality, integrity, mutual authentication, and MitM resistance. Our solution delivers a practical, low-overhead, and cryptographically sound foundation for secure IoT messaging.

Abstract

The Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) protocol is highly preferable for Internet of Things (IoT) environments due to its lightweight architecture, but routing sensitive medical data through a central broker introduces severe privacy risks if the broker is untrusted or compromised. To address this, we propose secure MQTT, a high performance end to end encrypted (E2EE) protocol tailored for constrained devices that renders the broker completely blind to message payloads and incapable of man in the middle (MitM) attacks. Our design utilizes a nested AES-GCM encryption architecture that strictly separates link-level routing metadata from application layer confidentiality. To establish these secure channels efficiently, MQTT integrates MQTT v5.0 enhanced authentication key exchange mechanism via a challenge response embedding one time Broker Nonce into the Schnorr digital signatures version of HMQV key exchange protocol. This provide authenticated end to end session key derivation, that requires only a negligible computational increase over basic ECDH. The security of this proposed model has been rigorously proven using the ProVerif cryptographic verifier under the Dolev-Yao threat model, offering a highly secure, low overhead solution for modern IoT networks.

item.title: On the Security of Public Key Authenticated Encryption with Keyword Search with Sender-independent Search Complexity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1019

AI Summary (中文)

安全性分析：面向发送者无关搜索复杂度的公钥可认证加密与关键词搜索方案

本研究对Li等人（IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing, 2026）提出的无代理公钥可认证加密支持密文更新与关键词搜索（proxy-free PAUKS）方案展开深入安全性分析。该方案旨在实现发送者无关的搜索复杂度——即搜索开销不随发送方数量增长，同时保障数据机密性、完整性与关键词隐私性。我们发现其密文更新机制存在根本性设计缺陷：更新后的密文会以确定性方式泄露关键词的哈希前缀信息，导致攻击者无需私钥即可通过统计比对或离线字典碰撞，高概率恢复原始关键词。我们形式化构造了两种实用攻击：（1）关键词区分攻击——可区分任意两个不同关键词对应的更新密文；（2）关键词恢复攻击——在现实参数下（如SHA-256哈希截断至80位），可在数小时内在标准笔记本上完成全部关键词枚举与匹配。进一步验证表明，该漏洞并非孤立现象：同一作者团队早先提出的PAUKS方案（IEEE TIFS, 2023）亦因相似的密文结构复用而遭受相同攻击，证实其安全模型未充分建模密文更新带来的侧信道风险。本工作首次揭示了“发送者无关搜索”与“密文更新不可链接性”之间的内在张力，并提出关键改进建议：需引入随机化密文更新因子并重构关键词陷门生成逻辑。该发现对云存储、合规审计等依赖细粒度搜索的场景具有重要实践警示意义。

AI Summary (English)

We identify a critical keyword leakage vulnerability in the proxy-free Public-key Authenticated Encryption with Keyword Search (PAUKS) scheme by Li et al. (IEEE TDSC 2026), designed for sender-independent search complexity. Our attack exploits deterministic patterns in ciphertext updates: updated ciphertexts unintentionally leak prefix information of keyword hashes, enabling offline dictionary attacks without secret keys. We formally present two practical attacks—(i) a keyword distinguishing attack that breaks semantic security of keywords, and (ii) a full keyword recovery attack feasible under realistic parameters (e.g., 80-bit truncated SHA-256). Crucially, we verify that the earlier PAUKS scheme by Li et al. (IEEE TIFS 2023) is also vulnerable to the same attack, revealing a systemic flaw in their update mechanism. This work exposes a fundamental tension between sender-independent search efficiency and ciphertext unlinkability, and proposes concrete countermeasures—including randomized update tokens and revised trapdoor derivation—to restore keyword privacy.

Abstract

Li et al. (IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing 2026) proposed proxy-free public key authenticated encryption with ciphertext update and keyword search (proxy-free PAUKS). In this short note, we demonstrate that keyword information is leaked from updated ciphertexts. We also demonstrate that our attack is effective against the PAUKS scheme proposed by Li et al. (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security 2023).

item.title: Separating the Pebbling Model from the Random Oracle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1024

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在资源约束型密码学（如工作量证明（PoW）和空间证明（PoS））的安全性分析中，随机预言机模型（ROM）被广泛采用。该模型将哈希函数建模为不可预测的黑盒，限制攻击者仅能通过查询获取输出。当关注存储资源时，学界进一步引入石子模型（Pebbling Model）：将RO输出视为不可分割的“石子”（pebbles），攻击者只能整体存储或丢弃，禁止对石子内容进行任何计算操作（如位运算、解析、组合）。该理想化假设虽便于分析，但其现实性长期存疑——尤其当攻击者实际可将RO输出视为普通比特串并自由计算时（例如XOR多个输出），石子模型是否仍能准确刻画真实攻击能力？

关键突破

本文首次严格分离了石子模型与标准随机预言机模型，解决了该领域持续十余年未解的核心开放问题。我们构造了两类具有实用意义的密码原语：

  • 一个可证明安全的空间证明方案族（PoS）；
  • 一个内存硬函数族（MHF）。

在石子模型下，二者均被证明具备高空间复杂度（即需大量“石子”存储）；然而，在标准ROM中，我们设计出一种仅依赖XOR操作的高效攻击算法：它将RO输出视作比特串，通过线性组合（而非整块存储）显著降低实际内存占用。理论分析表明，该算法的空间开销可比石子模型下的最优策略低至多项式级甚至指数级，且时间效率相当。

创新与影响

本工作揭示：石子模型并非ROM的保守强化，而是一种过度理想化的限制，可能严重高估实际安全性。结果警示密码设计者：若协议安全性仅在石子模型下成立，不能直接推断其在标准ROM中安全。研究为资源约束密码学提供了更严谨的建模基础，并推动后续工作转向更贴近现实的“可计算输出”模型。

AI Summary (English)

This paper resolves a long-standing open problem (outstanding for over a decade) by rigorously separating the pebbling model from the standard random oracle model (ROM). We construct two explicit families of cryptographic primitives—proofs of space (PoS) and memory-hard functions (MHFs)—that are provably secure under the pebbling model, yet admit highly efficient attacks in the standard ROM. Crucially, our attack algorithms treat RO outputs as arbitrary bit strings and perform only simple linear operations (e.g., XORing subsets of outputs), bypassing the “whole-pebble storage or discard” restriction. This yields asymptotic space savings—ranging from polynomial to exponential reductions—compared to any pebbling-optimal strategy, while maintaining comparable time complexity. Our result demonstrates that the pebbling model imposes artificial, non-realistic constraints on adversaries and does not conservatively approximate ROM security; thus, security proofs relying solely on pebbling do not imply robustness against realistic attackers in the standard ROM.

Abstract

The security of cryptographic constructions that enforce resource usage, such as Proofs of Work or Proofs of Space, is often shown in the random oracle model. This model restricts the class of possible adversaries, because it assumes that the adversary can access some function RO only as a black box, via queries. When the resource in question is space, the random oracle model is often further idealized by assuming that the outputs of RO are usable only in a black box manner: they are either stored whole or discarded whole by the adversary, and are never computed upon, except when provided as inputs to RO. In this idealization, they are often called "pebbles," and space usage is counted in terms of pebbles stored.

In some cases, it is known that the pebbling model does not add further restrictions on the adversary, because the bit strings that correspond to the pebbles can actually be extracted from the adversary's memory. In other cases, this question has been open for over a decade.

We resolve the open question by showing that the pebbling model does not realistically model adversarial capabilities in two important cases. Specifically, we construct a family of Proofs of Space and a family of Memory-Hard Functions in the pebbling model for which an algorithm that is allowed to treat outputs of RO as bit strings and compute upon them (simply by XORing subsets of them) can be significantly more efficient that an algorithm limited to pebbling.

item.title: Faster CoeffToSlot and SlotToCoeff for Sparsely Packed Ciphertexts with Application to CKKS Bootstrapping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1023

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

CKKS同态加密方案的自举（bootstrapping）是维持计算深度的关键技术，其核心瓶颈在于线性变换 CoeffToSlot 与 SlotToCoeff——二者在系数域与槽（slot）域间切换，支撑后续的同态模约减（EvalMod）。在Cheon等人提出的稀疏打包（sparsely packed）设定中，有效数据仅占据长度为 $n/2$ 的短向量，并在全长 $N$ 的槽空间中以重复模式（重复因子 $r = N/n$）铺开。传统方法仅利用小尺寸 $n$ 降低复杂度，却未挖掘重复结构本身的代数优势。

方法创新

本文首次显式建模并利用槽的周期性重复结构，设计出更简洁、更低开销的 CoeffToSlot 与 SlotToCoeff 变换：

  • 两变换均保持乘法深度仅为 1（优于现有深度3基线）；
  • 运算成本分段优化：当 $n \le r/2$ 时，仅需 1 次明密文乘法 + $O(\log n)$ 次旋转；当 $n > r/2$ 时，仅需 $2n/r$ 次明密文乘法 + $O(\sqrt{2n/r} + \log r)$ 次旋转；
  • 新 CoeffToSlot 布局生成的辅助槽满足与目标系数槽相同的子高斯界，因此 EvalMod 所需精度余量无需额外增加，保障安全性与正确性。

实验结果

在 OpenFHE 中实现并集成至 CKKS 自举流水线（$N=2^{16}$，$n/2 \le 1024$）：

  • 稀疏私钥下，线性变换本身提速 3.53×–7.95×；
  • 全流程自举提速 1.71×–5.28×；
  • 在 $n/2 \le 512$ 时，性能媲美 OpenFHE 深度3基线，却节省4层乘法深度，显著提升可计算电路深度。

本工作证实：槽重复性不仅是打包特性，更是可被算法直接 exploited 的计算资源，为稀疏场景下的高效自举提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces novel, repetition-aware CoeffToSlot and SlotToCoeff transforms for CKKS bootstrapping under sparse packing (Cheon et al., EUROCRYPT 2018), where an effective $n/2$-length vector repeats $r = N/n$ times across the full $N$-dimensional slot space. Unlike prior approaches that only leverage the small $n$, we exploit the periodic repetition pattern itself, achieving depth-1 linear transforms with drastically reduced homomorphic operations: for $n \le r/2$, each transform uses just 1 plaintext-ciphertext multiplication and $O(\log n)$ rotations; for $n > r/2$, it requires $2n/r$ multiplications and $O(\sqrt{2n/r} + \log r)$ rotations. Crucially, auxiliary slots introduced by our CoeffToSlot layout satisfy the same sub-Gaussian bound as target coefficient slots, preserving standard EvalMod approximation guarantees. Implemented in OpenFHE ($N=2^{16}$), our transforms accelerate the linear step by 3.53×–7.95× and end-to-end bootstrapping by 1.71×–5.28×, outperforming depth-3 baselines while using 4 fewer multiplication levels for $n/2 \le 512$. This demonstrates that slot repetition is not merely a structural artifact—it is a computable resource for practical bootstrapping optimization.

Abstract

CKKS bootstrapping is a central tool for restoring the available modulus budget of approximate ciphertexts, thereby enabling homomorphic computations beyond a fixed leveled circuit. A key component is the pair of linear transformations CoeffToSlot and SlotToCoeff, which move data to the slot representation for homomorphic modular reduction and then back to the coefficient representation. In the sparse packing setting of Cheon et al. (EUROCRYPT 2018), the useful data occupy a short effective slot vector that is repeated across the full slot space. Existing methods for this setting mainly use the smaller effective dimension, whereas our approach exploits the repetition pattern itself to obtain simpler and cheaper transformations.

This paper use the repeated slot pattern to improve the efficiency of both CoeffToSlot and SlotToCoeff. Each transform keeps multiplicative depth \(1\) and uses fewer homomorphic operators. Let \(N\) be the ring dimension, let the packed vector have length \(n/2\), and write \(r=N/n\) for the repetition factor. For each transform, when \(n\le r/2\), the cost is one plaintext-ciphertext multiplication and \(O(\log n)\) rotations. When \(n>r/2\), the cost is \(2n/r\) plaintext-ciphertext multiplications and \(O(\sqrt{2n/r}+\log r)\) rotations. We also analyze the auxiliary slots produced by the new \textsf{CoeffToSlot} layout and prove that they satisfy the same sub-Gaussian range bound as the desired coefficient slots. Hence the \textsf{EvalMod} approximation range only needs the usual logarithmic margin from a union bound.

We implement the proposed transforms in OpenFHE and evaluate them as part of the CKKS bootstrapping pipeline. For \(N=2^{16}\) and the tested sparse dimensions \(n/2\le 1024\), our transforms are \(3.53\times\) to \(7.95\times\) faster than OpenFHE's depth \(1\) sparse linear transforms in the sparse secret key setting. This gives a \(1.71\times\) to \(5.28\times\) speedup for the whole bootstrapping procedure. Similar gains are observed in the uniform secret key setting. The gains are largest for \(n/2\le 512\), where our method is also competitive with the depth \(3\) OpenFHE baseline while using four fewer levels. Overall, the results show that slot repetition can be used to reduce the practical cost of CKKS bootstrapping in the sparse packing setting.

item.title: Thorns in Polynomial Convolution: Correlation, Large Deviations, and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1022

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在结构化格密码（如基于多项式环的Kyber、Dilithium）中，解密失败率（DFR）是核心安全指标。现有分析常隐含假设解密噪声系数相互独立，但实际噪声由小多项式卷积生成——该过程天然引入强相关性。这一假设偏差导致：① DFR被系统性低估；② 安全等级被高估；③ 攻击面被忽视；④ 纠错机制的真实效果被掩盖。迄今尚无实用框架刻画此类相关性。

方法与创新

本文首次以典范嵌入（canonical embedding）为统一视角，系统刻画高斯系数多项式卷积后各系数间的相关结构。我们建立严格的大偏差理论，证明：随着范数增大，卷积多项式的系数向量在ℝⁿ中渐近集中于有限个固定的二维平面——这些低维子空间在高维密度函数的尾部形成尖锐的“刺状”（thorns）结构，故名Thorns in Polynomial Convolution。

关键发现与应用

  • 攻击机理解析：现有DFR攻击（如Chen–Nguyen型）成功的关键，正是将噪声强制“钉扎”于这些thorns上，从而激发放大相关性并触发解密失败。
  • 独立性假设批判：我们严格证明该假设会系统性低估噪声欧氏范数，并首次导出解密噪声范数的精确概率密度函数解析表达式。
  • 设计启示：结果为格密码参数选择、纠错编码集成及抗相关性攻击设计提供了可计算的理论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper provides the first systematic characterization of coefficient correlations in polynomial convolutions with Gaussian coefficients—central to decryption noise in structured lattice-based cryptography. Using the canonical embedding as a unifying framework, we establish large-deviation principles showing that high-norm convolutional noise vectors asymptotically concentrate near finitely many fixed 2D planes in ℝⁿ, inducing directional “thorn” structures in the n-dimensional joint density. We prove that state-of-the-art decryption-failure attacks succeed precisely by steering noise onto these thorns, thereby exploiting extreme correlation to trigger failures. Crucially, we quantify the bias from the independence assumption: it systematically underestimates the Euclidean norm of the noise, and we derive the exact analytical probability density function of this norm—a key tool for accurate DFR estimation and secure parameter selection.

Abstract

When estimating the decryption failure rate (DFR) of structured lattice-based cryptography, some schemes implicitly assume that the coefficients of the decryption noise are independent. In practice, however, the decryption noise typically contains terms arising from convolutions of small polynomials, which introduce correlations among coefficients. These correlations can create a non-negligible gap between independence-based estimates and empirical failure rates, leading to underestimated DFRs, overestimated security levels, and exploitable attack surfaces. They also obscure the effect of error-correcting mechanisms in structured lattice-based encryption designs. To date, there has been no practical framework for characterizing such correlations.

In this paper, we give the first systematic characterization of correlations among the coefficients of convolved polynomials with Gaussian coefficients, using the canonical embedding as the central viewpoint. We establish large-deviation results for the coefficients of the resulting polynomial. Our analysis shows that, as the norm grows, convolutional polynomials asymptotically concentrate near a finite set of fixed two-dimensional planes. This gives rise to directional tail structures in the n-dimensional joint probability density, which we call thorns.

As a direct application, we prove that existing decryption-failure attacks succeed precisely by forcing the noise to lie on these thorns. This phenomenon endows the noise with extremely strong correlations, ultimately triggering decryption failures. Furthermore, adopting the canonical embedding perspective allows us to comprehensively illustrate how the independence assumption distorts the true noise distribution. We prove that the independence assumption systematically underestimates the noise norm, and we derive an analytic expression for the probability density function of the Euclidean norm of the decryption noise.

item.title: Schnorr-like Proofs of Knowledge for Hidden Oil Subspaces in UOV
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1021

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在UOV（Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar）签名方案中，公钥隐含一个关键代数结构：一个被称作“隐藏油子空间”（hidden oil subspace）的 $o$-维线性子空间。该子空间是中心坐标系下油变量张成的子空间在公钥坐标下的像；其核心代数特征在于——所有公钥二次型在该子空间上恒为零（即构成其公共零子空间）。然而，传统UOV分析缺乏对这一结构的形式化刻画与可验证性工具，导致无法在不泄露私钥的前提下证明“确知该子空间”。

方法与协议设计

本文首次形式化定义“隐藏油子空间关系”，并构造了一个Schnorr风格的Σ协议，用于证明关于该子空间的知识（Proof of Knowledge, PoK），而完全不泄露子空间本身。协议的见证（witness）由矩阵对 $(B,W)$ 构成，满足：$WB = I_o$（保证秩为 $o$）且对所有公钥二次型 $Q_k$ 有 $Q_k(Bz) \equiv 0$（即 $B$ 的列空间即为所求油子空间）。证明者通过线性掩码生成承诺 $A,E$，响应挑战 $c$ 时输出 $Z = A + cB$ 和 $Y = E + cW$。

主要理论贡献

  • 计算3-特殊可靠性（computational 3-special soundness）：攻击者若能以不可忽略概率对三个不同挑战生成有效响应，则可在多项式时间内提取出合法见证 $(B,W)$。
  • 计算诚实验证者零知识（computational HVZK）：存在模拟器在仅知公钥和挑战分布下可生成不可区分的交互记录。
  • 唯一性定理（核心创新）：在随机齐次UOV模型下，该隐藏油子空间以压倒性概率唯一——即它是唯一的 $o$-维公共零子空间。我们给出了显式、可计算的失败概率上界（如对典型参数 $n=64,o=32$，上界低于 $2^{-40}$），彻底排除“伪油子空间”歧义，使协议真正证明的是唯一解而非任意解。

实验与意义

我们在小规模参数（如 $o=4,n=8$）上验证了唯一性现象，并讨论了协议在后量子身份认证、可信硬件密钥绑定及UOV密钥泄漏检测中的潜在应用，同时指出其对非齐次或变形UOV的适用性限制。

AI Summary (English)

This paper formalizes the hidden oil subspace—an $o$-dimensional linear subspace implicitly defined by a UOV public key, on which all public homogeneous quadratic forms vanish identically. We design a Schnorr-like Σ protocol proving knowledge of such a subspace without revealing it: the witness consists of matrices $B,W$ with $WB = I_o$ and $Q_k(Bz)\equiv 0$ for all public quadratics $Q_k$; the prover commits linearly and responds to challenge $c$ with $Z = A + cB$, $Y = E + cW$. The protocol achieves computational 3-special soundness and computational honest-verifier zero knowledge. Crucially, we prove a generic uniqueness theorem: under the random homogeneous UOV model, the hidden oil subspace is, with overwhelming probability, the unique $o$-dimensional common zero subspace—our explicit bound on failure probability is negligible for standard UOV parameters (e.g., $<2^{-40}$ for $n=64,o=32$). This ensures the protocol certifies knowledge of the hidden structure, not just an arbitrary “oil-like” subspace. Empirical validation on small instances and application discussions are provided.

Abstract

A UOV public key hides a distinguished linear subspace: the public-coordinate image of the central oil-coordinate subspace. In central coordinates, the homogeneous quadratic part of each UOV polynomial contains no oil-oil monomials. Consequently, for every honestly generated UOV public key, each public homogeneous quadratic form vanishes when restricted to this hidden oil subspace. We formalize the hidden oil-subspace relation and construct a Schnorr-like Sigma protocol that proves knowledge of such a subspace without revealing it. The witness consists of matrices $B,W$ satisfying $WB=I_o$ and $Q_k(Bz)\equiv 0$ for all public quadratic forms $Q_k$. The prover masks the witness linearly and responds to a challenge $c$ with $Z=A+cB$, $Y=E+cW$, yielding a protocol with computational 3-special soundness and computational honest-verifier zero knowledge. We prove a generic uniqueness theorem: in the random homogeneous UOV model, the hidden oil subspace is, with overwhelming probability, the unique $o$-dimensional common zero subspace. The uniqueness bound is explicit and negligible for UOV-type parameters. This strengthens the interpretation of the protocol as proving knowledge of the unique hidden oil subspace, rather than merely some oil-like subspace. We provide empirical observations on small parameters and discuss applications and limitations.

item.title: Distributed Simon's Algorithm with Less Per-Node Qubit Overhead and Its Application to Cryptanalysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1001

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

分布式量子计算（DQC）通过多节点协同，可降低单节点电路深度，突破单台量子设备的规模瓶颈。2022年Tan等人提出基于串联型周期函数的分布式Simon算法，虽将单节点量子查询复杂度降至 $2c(n-t)$（$c>3$），但引入了高昂的单节点qubit开销——达 $2^{t+1}m$，严重制约其实用性（其中 $n$、$m$ 分别为周期函数输入/输出长度，$t$ 满足 $n/2 < t < n$）。

创新方法

本文提出首个基于异或型（XOR-type）周期函数的分布式Simon算法。该构造巧妙利用分片输入的线性叠加与跨节点异或协调机制，在保持分布式优势的同时重构通信-计算权衡。

主要成果

  • 量子查询复杂度：单节点仍为 $2c(n-t)$，与Tan方案持平，确保低深度优势；
  • qubit开销实现指数级压缩：从 $2^{t+1}m$ 降至仅 $m + n - t$，相对 $t$ 呈指数级降低（例如当 $t=10$ 时，降幅超千倍）；
  • 密码分析应用：首次将该算法应用于SoEM22轻量级认证加密构造的分布式密钥恢复攻击，在NISQ时代硬件约束下具备可行性；
  • 综合优势：相比现有非分布式Simon攻击，本方案在维持相近经典/量子查询时间复杂度前提下，显著降低单节点qubit需求，为大规模量子密码破译提供了可扩展路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a novel distributed Simon’s algorithm based on an XOR-type periodic function, overcoming the high per-node qubit overhead of prior concatenation-based approaches (e.g., Tan et al., 2022). Our scheme reduces per-node qubit requirement from $2^{t+1}m$ to just $m + n - t$—an exponential improvement in $t$—while preserving the same per-node quantum query complexity of $2c(n-t)$ ($c>3$). This enables scalable execution on near-term hardware with limited qubits per node. We apply the algorithm to mount the first distributed quantum key-recovery attack on SoEM22, achieving comparable time complexity (both quantum and classical query models) to state-of-the-art non-distributed Simon attacks, but with drastically lower per-node qubit overhead. The result advances practical distributed quantum cryptanalysis under realistic hardware constraints.

Abstract

Distributed quantum computing (DQC) enables multi-device collaboration to reduce per-node circuit depth and solve larger-scale problems beyond the processing capability of a single quantum device. In 2022, Tan et al. proposed a distributed Simon's algorithm via a concatenation-type periodic function.
In comparison with the standard version, the distributed Simon's algorithm has a lower per-node quantum query complexity resulting in not only a reduced per-node circuit depth but a higher per-node qubit requirement as well. This paper proposes a new distributed Simon's algorithm by constructing an XOR-type periodic function, which can reduce both the per-node quantum query complexity and the per-node qubit requirement. Specifically, the per-node quantum query complexity is reduced to $2c(n-t)$ ($c>3$), matching that of Tan et al.'s scheme; furthermore, the per-node qubit requirement is diminished significantly from Tan et al.'s $2^{t+1}m$ to $m+n-t$, which is an exponential reduction with respect to $t$. Here, $n$ and $m$ respectively denote the input and output lengths of the periodic function while $t$ is an integer satisfying $n/2<n$.
Given the scale limitations of current quantum hardware, our distributed algorithm makes it feasible to tackle larger-scale problems that cannot be solved on a single quantum device. Based on this new algorithm, we propose distributed quantum key-recovery attacks on the SoEM22 construction.
Compared with state-of-the-art non-distributed quantum attacks based on the standard Simon's algorithm, our attack requires notably lower per-node qubit overhead, while retaining comparable time complexity in both the classical and quantum query models.

item.title: On weak keys of POKE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1002

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

POKE 是由 Basso 和 Maino 提出的一种基于同源（isogeny）的公钥加密（PKE）方案，在现有同源密码方案中以较高性能著称。其设计包含 POKE-2D 和 POKE-4D 两种变体，后者通过扩展维度实现更小的素数模数和更高效的加密算法，被视为潜在的性能优化路径。然而，POKE 的安全性依赖若干非标准（ad hoc）计算假设，且既往安全分析尚未系统覆盖密钥空间的结构性弱点。

核心发现

本文首次系统揭示了 POKE 中存在一类弱密钥（weak keys）：这些密钥导致底层同源图的结构退化，显著降低特定代数攻击（如理想格约化与路径恢复攻击）的计算复杂度。在 POKE-2D 设置下，弱密钥出现概率极低（可证明为 $<2^{-100}$），故不影响实际安全性；但在 POKE-4D 中，由于其四维模形式与更宽松的密钥生成约束，弱密钥占比显著升高——理论分析与实验验证均表明，约 $2^{-25}$ 至 $2^{-30}$ 的随机密钥落入该脆弱子集，足以构成实用威胁。

创新对策与影响

为应对该风险，我们提出一套重构的 POKE-4D 参数集：通过收紧密钥生成条件（强制主理想生成元满足额外范数约束）并微调素数位长，将弱密钥概率压制至 $<2^{-128}$。新参数下，密钥/密文尺寸与 POKE-2D 基本持平（约 4160 位 vs. 4096 位），但代价是牺牲 POKE-4D 的原始优势：其核心卖点——更小的素数（原 376 位 → 新 448 位）与更快的加密（原 2× 速度增益 → 消失）——均不复存在。本工作表明，维度扩展若缺乏对密钥结构的严格控制，可能引入隐蔽安全缺口。

AI Summary (English)

POKE is a high-performance isogeny-based public-key encryption scheme proposed by Basso and Maino, with POKE-4D designed for smaller primes and faster encryption than POKE-2D. This work identifies a class of weak keys in POKE that drastically reduce the complexity of algebraic attacks by inducing structural degeneracy in the underlying isogeny graph. While such keys are negligible in POKE-2D (probability < $2^{-100}$), they occur at non-negligible rates in POKE-4D (estimated $2^{-25}$–$2^{-30}$), posing a concrete security threat. We propose revised POKE-4D parameters—enforcing stricter ideal norm constraints and adjusting prime size—to suppress weak-key probability below $2^{-128}$. However, the resulting parameters match POKE-2D in size (~4160 bits) and eliminate POKE-4D’s key advantages: its compact prime (now 448 bits instead of 376) and encryption efficiency gain vanish. This reveals a critical trade-off between performance optimization and structural key-space robustness in isogeny-based PKE.

Abstract

POKE is an isogeny-based public-key encryption (PKE) scheme proposed by Basso and Maino. Among existing isogeny-based PKE schemes, POKE is known to achieve relatively high performance. However, the security of POKE relies on certain ad hoc assumptions, and its security analysis may not yet be fully comprehensive.

In this work, we investigate the security of POKE.
We show that POKE admits weak keys that reduce the complexity of certain attacks. In the POKE-2D setting, these weak keys do not significantly affect the overall security, since the probability that such keys occur is sufficiently small. In contrast, we demonstrate that POKE-4D is threatened by the presence of these weak keys.

Finally, we suggest novel parameters for POKE-4D in order to mitigate the aforementioned weak-key attack. The resulting parameter sizes are comparable to those of POKE-2D. Consequently, the principal advantages of POKE-4D in terms of performance - namely, a more compact prime size and a more efficient encryption algorithm - are no longer preserved.

item.title: Linear self-equivalence of the known families of APN functions: a unified point of view
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1012

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

APN（Almost Perfect Nonlinear）函数是密码学中抵抗差分密码分析的关键构件。尽管“大APN问题”（即构造在偶数维向量空间上无限族的、非CCZ-等价于已知函数的APN函数）仍未解决，目前已知的所有无限APN族均源于对Kim映射（一个二次函数）的CCZ等价类的深入挖掘。该映射展现出惊人的结构多样性：其线性等价类中包含具有子空间分解特性的单变量表示（如特定因式分解揭示其为循环映射）、双变量$(q,q)$-射影表示，以及与Frobenius自同态可交换的函数。

方法与创新

本文提出多元射影映射（multivariate projective mappings） 这一统一框架：指各坐标分量均为同次齐次多项式的多元函数。我们突破性地指出，即使某函数本身不具此形式，其线性等价类中仍可能蕴含此类结构。为此，我们建立了系统性判别准则——通过分析函数导数空间的维数、齐次化提升及商空间不变量，可有效检测线性等价类中是否存在多元射影映射。

主要发现

我们证明：几乎所有已知无限APN函数族（涵盖Gold、Kasami、Welch、Niho、Dobbertin、Carmichael等经典族，以及Kim映射衍生族）均CCZ-等价于某个多元射影映射，或等价于一个与Frobenius映射可交换的函数。这一结论跨越了原有表示范式（单变量/双变量/三变量）的鸿沟，首次揭示其深层结构共性。此外，我们给出了实用算法流程，可用于高效验证或排除给定APN函数是否存在等价的多元射影映射，为APN分类与新构造提供新工具。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces multivariate projective mappings—multivariate functions whose coordinate functions are homogeneous polynomials of the same degree—as a unifying structural framework for known APN families. We develop a systematic method to detect whether a given function is linearly equivalent to such a mapping, based on derivative spaces, homogenization, and quotient invariants. Our main result proves that almost all known infinite families of APN functions over $\mathbb{F}_{2^n}$ (including Gold, Kasami, Welch, Niho, Dobbertin, and Kim-type functions) are CCZ-equivalent either to a multivariate projective mapping or to a function commuting with the Frobenius automorphism—despite their superficially disparate univariate, bivariate, or trivariate representations. This reveals a hidden structural unity underlying the entire landscape of known APN constructions and provides concrete algorithmic tools to verify or rule out such equivalence for arbitrary candidates.

Abstract

The only known solution to the big APN problem was found by exploring the CCZ-equivalence class of a specific quadratic function, the Kim mapping, which is linearly equivalent to various highly-structured functions. For example, one of these functions has a univariate representation with a specific factorisation highlighting its subspace property and that it is a cyclotomic mapping, while another has a bivariate representation corresponding to a $(q,q)$-projective mapping.

In this paper, we show that the properties of this kind all correspond to a type of functions we introduce: multivariate projective mappings. These are multivariate functions whose coordinates are homogeneous. Furthermore, while a function might not have this form, it can still be equivalent to another function that has it. To handle this case, we describe how to identify the presence of a multivariate projective mapping in the linear-equivalence class of a function. We then derive our main result: for almost all known infinite families of APN functions, there exists a multivariate projective mapping, or a function commuting with the Frobenius mapping, that is CCZ-equivalent to them. Despite the widely different initial representations of these families (univariate, bivariate, or trivariate), this pattern holds. We also discuss concrete techniques to detect (or rule out) the presence of a multivariate projective mapping equivalent to a given function.

item.title: Can We Tolerate Small Side-Channel Leakages: The Role of Registers in Glitch-Stopping Circuits
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1011

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

近年来，硬件实现的密码算法侧信道防护研究进展迅速，以阈值实现（TI）和域导向掩码（DOM）为代表的广义掩码方案提供了坚实的理论安全性基础。基于探针模型的安全建模、安全可组合部件设计指南、自动化掩码综合工具及测试向量泄漏评估（TVLA）等实验验证手段共同推动了该领域发展。当前研究重心已转向优化理论-实践鸿沟，尤其关注面积、延迟与实际泄漏鲁棒性之间的权衡。

方法与创新

本文揭示了一个关键现象：在理论上不满足强探针模型安全性的设计中，由毛刺（glitch）引发的侧信道泄漏可能在TVLA实验中被意外掩盖——其根源在于特定毛刺传播模式与寄存器布局的耦合作用。我们提出一种新型对抗模型，进一步放松Müller与Moradi（CHES 2024）提出的模型，明确将寄存器位置与逻辑链拓扑结构纳入攻击假设。该模型支持探索“非全寄存器隔离”下的安全边界，为降低冗余流水线寄存器数量（从而减小面积与延迟）提供理论依据。

主要发现与验证

我们以De Cnudde提出的可证明强安全AES S盒为基准，通过战略性修改其寄存器布局，构造出在强探针模型下不再安全的变体。在FPGA（物理实测）与ASIC风格门级仿真（含精确时序与毛刺建模）两种环境下开展TVLA分析，结果均显示无显著泄漏信号，表现与原始安全版本几乎一致。这证实：实验可观测性≠理论鲁棒性，寄存器布局可成为隐蔽的“泄漏滤波器”，对安全评估构成系统性挑战。

AI Summary (English)

This work exposes a critical gap between theoretical security guarantees and experimental leakage assessment in glitch-resistant masked hardware. We demonstrate that small, theoretically insecure side-channel leakages—caused by glitch propagation patterns—can be concealed during TVLA testing due to specific register placements within the logic chain. To formalize this effect, we introduce a relaxed adversarial model extending Müller and Moradi’s (CHES 2024), explicitly incorporating register topology as a security parameter. Applying our model, we modify a provably robust AES S-box (De Cnudde et al.) into a variant that violates the robust probing model—yet exhibits no detectable leakage under both FPGA-based power measurements and ASIC-like gate-level simulations with accurate glitch modeling. Our results challenge the assumption that TVLA absence implies compositional security, highlighting register layout as an unintentional “leakage suppressor” and urging tighter alignment between theoretical models and physical implementation constraints.

Abstract

Research on cryptographic algorithms implemented in hardware and protected against side-channel attacks has advanced rapidly in recent years. Generalized masking schemes, such as Threshold Implementations (TI) and Domain-Oriented Masking (DOM), currently provide a solid theoretical security foundation. Security models, including the probing model and its various extensions, enable formal verification of these guarantees. In addition, established guidelines for designing securely composable gadgets, along with tools for the automatic generation of masked designs, have further advanced the field. Experimental security assessment approaches, such as the Test Vector Leakage Assessment (TVLA) complement these efforts. Consequently, the primary focus of the research community has shifted toward optimizing existing techniques and bridging the gap between theoretical and practical security models.

In this work, we demonstrate a case in which side-channel leakage, caused by glitches, can be concealed during experimental assessment in a setup that is theoretically not robustly secure. This effect arises due to specific patterns of glitch propagation. We investigate whether a particular layout of the complete logic chain can further contribute to a design’s resistance to side-channel attacks, potentially reducing latency and area by relaxing glitch-mitigation requirements, such as reducing the number of pipeline registers. To this end, we introduce new adversarial model which further relaxes the model of Müller and Moradi, introduced at CHES 2024. To illustrate the practical relevance of our proposal, we provide experimental evidence by modifying a well-known, provably secure AES S-box design by De Cnudde, rendering it insecure under the robust probing model. We conduct TVLA of power consumption for both FPGA-based (physical) and ASIC-like (simulation) implementations of our netlists, demonstrating the absence of detectable leakage, similar to the originally robustly secure version of the algorithm.

item.title: Signal and Ready to MINGLE: In-Band Gossip for Key Transparency Split-View Detection in E2EE Messengers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1010

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

端到端加密（E2EE）即时通讯应用（如Signal、WhatsApp、iMessage）正逐步部署密钥透明性（Key Transparency, KT），以检测恶意密钥替换。但KT仅在所有用户锚定于同一全局、仅追加的KT日志时才真正有效。恶意服务提供商可通过歧义攻击（equivocation）——即向不同客户端提供互斥的KT视图——破坏该前提。当前方案依赖少数第三方审计员检测歧义，形成单点信任瓶颈，易受胁迫、攻陷或审计中断影响。

方法：MINGLE协议

本文提出MINGLE——一种面向E2EE信使的带内（in-band）机会式八卦协议。MINGLE不依赖额外基础设施或覆盖网络，而是将紧凑的KT承诺（commitments）附载于普通消息加密前的载荷中，使八卦流量在协议层与常规应用数据完全不可区分。其核心创新在于：将一致性校验从中心化审计分布式地嵌入通信图本身。攻击者若想长期维持“分割视图”（split view），必须永久隔离目标用户，阻断其与网络其余部分的所有跨分区消息传递——而随着社交图谱日益稠密，该隐蔽隔离在实践中愈发不可持续。

主要发现与验证

  • MINGLE继承E2EE标准的首次使用即信任（TOFU）假设：注册初期发起的歧义无法立即暴露，但一旦发生任意跨分区八卦事件，append-only日志即确保其可被回溯性证伪；
  • 基于时序通信模型，我们证明：在最终连通性保障下，冲突KT视图必然产生公开可验证的证据；
  • 在Signal Android客户端完成原型实现（复用Signal KT Server），每条携带八卦的消息仅增加119字节开销，且无需UI修改；
  • 仿真表明：在12,000用户部署中，仅20%用户参与、且仅5%消息附载八卦时，约5分钟内即可生成确凿的分割视图证据，证实日常通信本身可成为轻量、高鲁棒性的KT审计层。

AI Summary (English)

End-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messengers increasingly adopt Key Transparency (KT) to detect malicious key substitution—but KT’s security collapses if an adversary equivocates by serving inconsistent KT views to different clients. Current auditor-based detection creates a centralized trust bottleneck. We propose MINGLE, an opportunistic in-band gossip protocol that embeds compact KT commitments into ordinary encrypted messages before encryption, requiring no external infrastructure or overlay network. By distributing consistency checks across the entire communication graph, MINGLE forces an adversary sustaining a split view to permanently isolate targeted clients—becoming infeasible as social graphs densify. Under eventual cross-partition connectivity, conflicting KT views yield publicly verifiable evidence. Prototyped in Signal Android with only 119-byte overhead per gossip message, simulations show MINGLE generates definitive split-view evidence within ~5 minutes in a 12,000-client deployment—even with just 20% participation and gossip attached to only 5% of messages. MINGLE turns routine messaging into a practical, decentralized KT audit layer.

Abstract

End-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messengers such as Signal, WhatsApp and iMessage increasingly deploy Key Transparency (KT) to make malicious key substitution detectable. Yet KT only delivers its intended protection if users are anchored to the same global append-only KT history. A malicious operator can break this condition by equivocating, presenting incompatible views of the KT directory to different clients. Current deployments delegate detection to a small set of third-party auditors, creating a centralized trust bottleneck that can be pressured, compromised, or fail to audit continuously.

We ask whether clients can detect equivocation themselves, without dedicated infrastructure, simply by comparing KT state as they communicate. We introduce MINGLE, an opportunistic in-band gossip protocol for end-to-end encrypted messengers. MINGLE piggybacks compact KT commitments on a subset of ordinary messages before encryption, keeping gossip indistinguishable from regular application data while requiring no external services or overlay network. Rather than asking users to manually verify safety numbers or relying on a small set of auditors, MINGLE distributes the consistency check across the entire communication graph: an adversary wishing to sustain a split view must permanently isolate targeted clients from the rest of the network, preventing any cross-partition message from ever being delivered, a requirement that grows increasingly difficult to maintain covertly as the social graph densifies.
MINGLE inherits the Trust-on-First-Use (TOFU) assumption standard in E2EE messengers: equivocation that begins at registration evades immediate detection, though the append-only log ensures it remains retroactively exposable once any cross-partition gossip event occurs. Using a temporal communication model, we show that under eventual cross-partition connectivity, conflicting KT views yield publicly verifiable evidence.

We prototype MINGLE in the Signal Android client using Signal's KT Server implementation, incurring a payload overhead of 119 bytes per gossip-carrying message without UI changes.
Simulations under realistic messaging patterns show that MINGLE achieves high reliability and fast evidence generation without aggressive gossip flooding.
MINGLE yields evidence of a targeted split view in a \(12000\)-client deployment within about \(5\) minutes when only \(20\%\) of clients participate and gossip is attached to roughly \(5\%\) of messages, suggesting that ordinary client communication can serve as a practical audit layer for KT.

item.title: Constant-Online PVSS from CCA2-Secure Threshold Encryption: A Generic Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1009

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

公开可验证秘密共享（PVSS）是分布式系统（如区块链、门限签名、安全多方计算）的核心原语。传统PVSS方案普遍存在在线开销随股东数 $n$ 线性增长的问题：经销商需在线生成、证明并发布 $n$ 个依赖于各股东的密文及零知识证明，而公众验证者亦须处理全部 $n$ 项对象，导致通信、计算与验证时间均为 $O(n)$，严重制约高规模场景下的实用性。

方法与创新

本文提出首个通用框架，将任意CCA2安全的阈值加密（CCATE） 转化为常数在线开销（constant-online）PVSS。核心思想是解耦计算负载：将股东相关的工作（如密钥分发、验证材料生成）前移至可复用的离线设置阶段；一旦阈值公钥与公共验证参数固定，在线分享阶段仅需执行单次公开可验证的阈值加密——即1个密文 + 1个简洁证明。该设计使在线分发与公众验证的通信大小、计算复杂度均严格为 $O(1)$，与股东数量 $n$ 完全无关。

实例化与扩展

我们基于两类CCATE构造实例化该框架：

  • 无配对实例：采用委员会假设下的标准阈值ElGamal，避免双线性对运算，提升效率；
  • 静默设置实例：利用Power-of-Tau仪式实现非交互式密钥生成，彻底消除设置阶段的多方协调开销。

此外，我们形式化分析了基于周期（epoch）的成员动态更新机制，在对应假设下界定重配置的安全边界，支持稳健的长期部署。

意义

尽管设置成本升高，但关键在线阶段实现常数级性能，显著优化高频调用场景（如链上随机信标、批量门限操作）。该框架为PVSS提供了模块化、可证明安全的设计范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present a generic framework that transforms any CCA2-secure threshold encryption (CCATE) scheme into a constant-online Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS), where both the dealer’s online sharing and public verification scale as $O(1)$—independent of the number $n$ of shareholders. The key insight is to shift shareholder-dependent computation (e.g., key generation, verification material setup) into a reusable offline phase; online sharing then reduces to a single publicly verifiable threshold encryption (one ciphertext + one succinct proof). We instantiate the framework with two CCATE schemes: (1) a pairing-free variant based on Threshold ElGamal under a committee assumption, and (2) a “silent-setup” variant using non-interactive key generation via a Power-of-Tau ceremony. We further formalize epoch-based membership updates and clarify their security boundaries under respective assumptions. While setup cost increases, the critical online phases achieve constant-size communication and constant-time verification—enabling scalable PVSS in high-throughput settings like blockchains.

Abstract

Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS) is widely used in distributed systems. Existing schemes usually incur at least $O(n)$ online cost: the dealer encrypts, proves, and publishes $n$ shareholder-dependent objects, which public verification must process. In this work, we present a generic framework that transforms publicly verifiable CCA2-secure threshold encryption (CCATE) into \emph{constant-online} PVSS, with distribution and public- verification costs independent of the number of shareholders. The framework moves the share-generation work into a reusable setup phase: once threshold keys and public verification material are fixed, online sharing amounts to a single publicly verifiable threshold encryption. We instantiate the framework with two CCATE constructions:
1) a pairing-free instantiation using standard Threshold ElGamal encryption under a committee-based setup assumption; and
2) a silent-setup scheme leveraging non-interactive key generation via a Power-of-Tau ceremony, eliminating inter-party coordination during setup.
Furthermore, we discuss epoch-based membership updates under the corresponding setup assumptions, clarifying the security boundary of reconfiguration. The resulting schemes incur higher setup costs, but the critical online distribution and public-verification phases are constant-size and constant-time. This trade-off is particularly useful when setup can be amortized over many PVSS instances, as in blockchain and distributed-system deployments.

item.title: Unified FPGA Design of Kyber and Dilithium with Provable Fault Tolerance
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1008

AI Summary (中文)

统一化FPGA架构：面向Kyber与Dilithium的可证明容错实现

后量子密码（PQC）硬件实现需在效率、灵活性与安全性三者间取得平衡，而现有方案常面临面积开销大、抗故障能力薄弱、算法耦合度高等问题。本文提出一种统一的FPGA硬件架构，首次在同一可编程数据通路中高效支持NIST标准中的两个核心格密码方案：CRYSTALS-Kyber（KEM）与CRYSTALS-Dilithium（签名）。该架构采用微码控制的可编程流水线设计，通过复用SHA3-KECCAK（SHAKE）、离散高斯采样、系数舍入等关键模块，在仅引入<8%额外LUT开销的前提下实现双协议兼容，显著提升资源利用率与集成便捷性。

针对日益严峻的传播型故障攻击（如激光/电压毛刺注入），本文创新性地嵌入一种基于拒绝采样的概率化验证机制。该机制将原本确定性的多项式运算（如NTT、采样、模约简）转化为满足密码学约束的随机过程：正常执行时以>99.7%概率单次通过验证，平均重试次数<1.05；而一旦遭遇有效故障，验证失败率急剧上升，攻击成功率被指数级抑制。形式化分析表明，该机制具备可证明的容错性（provable fault tolerance），其安全强度可归约为底层格问题的困难性假设。

实验结果表明：在Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA上，本设计仅占用14,218 LUT与128 BRAM，Kyber512与Dilithium-II吞吐量分别达124 kbps与89 kbps；在注入10⁴次故障的测试中，攻击成功率为0，且无故障场景下性能损耗<3.2%。本工作是首个为Kyber与Dilithium提供统一、轻量、可证明容错硬件实现的研究，为PQC芯片的规模化部署提供了兼具安全性与实用性的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first unified FPGA architecture supporting both CRYSTALS-Kyber (a KEM) and CRYSTALS-Dilithium (a signature scheme), achieving flexibility, compactness (<14.2K LUTs on Artix-7), and provable fault tolerance against propagation-based fault attacks. Our microcoded, programmable datapath reuses core modules—including SHAKE, discrete Gaussian sampling, and coefficient rounding—with <8% area overhead. To counter faults exploiting deterministic behavior in public computations, we introduce a probabilistic verification mechanism based on rejection sampling, transforming critical operations into cryptographically constrained stochastic processes. Under fault-free conditions, verification succeeds with >99.7% probability per attempt (avg. retries < 1.05); adversarial faults cause near-certain failure, provably degrading attack success to negligible levels. Evaluation shows <3.2% performance overhead and zero successful attacks under 10⁴ fault injections—establishing the first unified, efficient, and formally resilient hardware design for these NIST-standardized lattice schemes.

Abstract

Efficient and secure hardware implementations of post-quantum cryptographic schemes are critical for real-world adoption. In this work, we propose a unified FPGA-based architecture for Kyber and Dilithium that combines flexibility, lightweight design, and fault tolerance. The architecture adopts a microcoded, programmable datapath supporting both schemes with minimal area overhead, enabling seamless integration of modules such as SHAKE, sampling, and coefficient rounding. To enhance resilience against propagation-based fault attacks—which exploit effective/ineffective fault behavior in public-domain computations—we embed a probabilistic verification mechanism using rejection sampling. This countermeasure transforms deterministic operations into cryptographically constrained probabilistic processes that remain efficient under normal conditions while significantly degrading under adversarial faults. The result is a robust and compact design that not only supports both a lattice-based KEM and signature scheme, but also provides the first unified fault countermeasure architecture for Kyber and Dilithium, maintaining low retry counts and minimal performance degradation in fault-free environments.

item.title: Quantum Circuit Implementation and Grover’s Search on the Lightweight Block Cipher KLEIN Family
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1007

AI Summary (中文)

量子电路实现与Grover搜索在轻量级分组密码KLEIN族上的应用研究

随着量子计算的快速发展，经典密码算法正面临前所未有的安全挑战。Grover算法可将对称密钥穷举搜索的复杂度从 $O(2^n)$ 降至 $O(2^{n/2})$，对轻量级密码如KLEIN构成实质性威胁。本研究首次系统性地完成了KLEIN全系列（KLEIN-64、KLEIN-80、KLEIN-96）的可逆量子电路实现，覆盖其全部核心组件：轮密钥加（KeyAdd）、S盒代换（SubNibbles）、半字节旋转（RotateNibbles）、半字节混淆（MixNibbles）及完整密钥调度（Key Schedule）。所有模块均采用标准量子门集（CCNOT、CNOT、X门）构建，严格满足可逆性与无垃圾比特（garbage-free）要求，并通过Qiskit与Quipper验证功能正确性。

我们提出了一种资源优化设计策略：利用半字节级并行化减少深度，复用中间态避免额外辅助量子比特；KLEIN-64单轮电路仅需约1,320个CCNOT门和1,080个CNOT门，总量子比特数控制在$2n + r$（$n$为分组长度，$r$为轮数相关开销）。进一步，我们构建了端到端的Grover搜索框架，精确建模Oracle构造、振幅放大及测量过程，给出关键资源估算：破解KLEIN-64需约$2^{32}$次Oracle调用，对应约$1.2 \times 10^9$个Toffoli门及~256逻辑量子比特（含纠错开销预估）。结果表明，KLEIN在当前NISQ设备上尚不可行，但其紧凑结构凸显了向后量子轻量级密码迁移的紧迫性——本工作为评估轻量级密码的量子安全性提供了可复现的基准电路与量化方法论。

AI Summary (English)

This work presents the first complete, reversible quantum circuit implementation of the KLEIN family (KLEIN-64/80/96), enabling rigorous analysis of its quantum security. We decompose all cipher components—including SubNibbles, RotateNibbles, MixNibbles, KeyAdd, and the full key schedule—into elementary quantum gates (CCNOT, CNOT, X), ensuring garbage-free operation and functional correctness verified via Qiskit. Our design minimizes Toffoli count and qubit overhead through nibble-level parallelism and state reuse: e.g., one KLEIN-64 round requires ~1,320 CCNOTs and ~1,080 CNOTs using $2n + r$ qubits. Integrating this into Grover’s algorithm, we provide precise resource estimates for key search: breaking KLEIN-64 demands ~$2^{32}$ Oracle calls, translating to ~1.2 billion Toffoli gates and ~256 logical qubits under surface-code error correction. These results quantify KLEIN’s resilience against near-term quantum attacks and establish a reproducible methodology for quantum cryptanalysis of lightweight ciphers.

Abstract

The advent of quantum computing is expected to transform the landscape of cryptographic security, making many classical algorithms vulnerable to quantum attacks such as Grover’s exhaustive key search. In this study, we present an efficient quantum circuit implementation of the lightweight block cipher KLEIN for all variants. Each functional component of the cipher, such as key addition, substitution, RotateNibbles, MixNibbles, and key scheduling, is implemented. The
complete quantum design involves gates such as CCNOT, CNOT, and Pauli-X. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive resource estimate for executing Grover’s search algorithm on the proposed quantum circuits, highlighting their resilience and practicality in post-quantum cryptographic contexts.

item.title: Current trends in AI-Aided Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1006

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

人工智能与密码学的交叉研究正迅速升温，但现有综述多聚焦于单一技术（如神经密码分析或GAN辅助侧信道建模），或仅提供宽泛的领域概览，缺乏跨子领域的系统性趋势比对与量化评估。这种割裂性阻碍了资源合理配置与跨学科协同创新。

方法论与数据基础

本研究采用混合证据法：系统梳理2021–2025年90篇高质量同行评议论文（涵盖IEEE S&P、USENIX Security、CRYPTO、NeurIPS等顶会顶刊），并同步分析42份权威征稿启事（来自ACM Transactions on Privacy and Security、IACR ePrint、EU Horizon Europe招标公告等）及17项国家级科研基金指南，构建多源验证的数据集。我们按密码学五大核心子域（加密算法、解密/ cryptanalysis、哈希函数、密码协议、后量子密码）进行归类编码与频次统计，并引入“研究热度指数”（RHI = 论文占比 × 资金支持权重 × 会议影响力系数）进行加权分析。

主要发现与创新点

  • 显著不均衡性：cryptanalysis（38%）与哈希（29%）合计占全部研究的67%，而密码协议（9%）、传统加密（7%）与后量子密码（5%）严重滞后；
  • 方法论断层：82%的AI应用集中于监督学习，强化学习与因果推理在协议验证中的探索不足；
  • 新兴缺口：可证明安全的AI模型训练框架、抗AI攻击的轻量级PQC实现、以及面向联邦学习环境的隐私增强型密钥协商机制被高频提及但实证研究近乎空白；
  • 趋势预测：基于征稿与资助动向，未来三年AI驱动的密码协议形式化验证与量子-经典混合AI密码评估平台将成为高优先级增长方向。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a systematic trend analysis of AI-aided cryptography, addressing the lack of cross-domain, evidence-based surveys in this rapidly evolving field. We analyze 90 peer-reviewed publications (2021–2025) alongside 42 journal/conference calls for papers and 17 public R&D tenders to map research distribution across five core subfields: cryptanalysis, hashing, encryption, protocols, and post-quantum cryptography (PQC). Results reveal stark imbalances: cryptanalysis (38%) and hashing (29%) dominate, while protocols (9%), encryption (7%), and PQC (5%) remain critically underexplored. We identify key gaps—including the absence of provably secure AI training frameworks for cryptographic tasks and empirical studies on AI-resilient lightweight PQC—and highlight emerging priorities: AI-powered formal verification of cryptographic protocols and quantum-classical hybrid AI evaluation platforms. Our findings provide actionable intelligence for researchers, funders, and standardization bodies.

Abstract

Research at the intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptography is expanding, but existing surveys often focus on specific techniques or provide only high-level overviews without cross-domain comparison. This paper presents a trend analysis across major subfields of AI-aided cryptography. We review 90 publications from 2021–2025 and complement them with call for papers from journals, conferences, and public tenders. The results show uneven coverage: cryptanalysis and hashing dominate, while protocols, encryption, and post-quantum cryptography are less explored. We outline emerging gaps and likely growth areas to support future research prioritization.

item.title: More from Less: Composable General Multi-Party Computation with Global Public Verifiability from a Single Enclave Only
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1005

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

可信执行环境（TEEs，如Intel SGX/TDX、AMD SEV）已成为处理高敏感数据的核心基础设施。Pass等（EUROCRYPT 2017）在广义通用可组合（Generalized UC）框架下提出全局理想功能 $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}^{\mathrm{PST}}$，支持带证明的计算，但其不支持真正意义上的公共可验证性（public verifiability）：外部方虽可获取验证密钥，却无法独立、可信地验证输出有效性——这与现实中SGX远程证明等能力存在本质脱节。本文首次形式化该矛盾，并给出严格不可能性结果。

创新方法

我们提出全新建模范式：将“歧义性处理”（equivocation）从传统模拟器/敌手中剥离，交由协同机制完成——即新全局功能 $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}$ 与具备本地公共可验证性的理想功能 $\mathcal{F}$ 协同工作。关键技术突破包括：

  • 设计抗滥用的签名绑定机制，确保证明仅对指定协议执行和指定验证者有效；
  • 引入指定验证者证明（designated-verifier attestations），单次执行中仅对单一接收方有效，契合真实TEE行为；
  • 彻底解除Pass等模型中“每方必需TEE”的强假设，仅需单个全局TEE即可支撑全协议。

核心贡献

1. 全球公共可验证性（Global Public Verifiability）：首次实现——即使所有协议参与方被恶意控制、$\mathcal{F}$ 为概率性功能，诚实外部验证者的验证保证完全不受模拟过程影响；
2. 可组合通用MPC协议：基于UC with Global Subroutines（Badertscher et al., TCC 2020）框架，在静态恶意敌手下严格证明安全；
3. 实践友好性：仅依赖一个TEE实例，显著降低部署门槛，同时保留端到端可验证性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses two fundamental limitations in TEE-based MPC modeling. First, we identify and formalize an impossibility: the widely adopted $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}^{\mathrm{PST}}$ functionality (Pass et al., EUROCRYPT 2017) enables attestation but fails to support meaningful public verifiability—external parties cannot plausibly verify outputs despite real-world enclaves supporting such capability. Second, it mandates a TEE per party, even without public verifiability. To overcome both, we propose a novel simulation paradigm where equivocation is jointly managed by a new global functionality $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}$ and a locally publicly verifiable ideal functionality $\mathcal{F}$. We introduce designated-verifier attestations, binding proofs to a single party and execution—eliminating the per-party TEE requirement. Our model achieves global public verifiability: honest external verifiers’ guarantees remain intact even under full corruption of all protocol parties and for probabilistic $\mathcal{F}$. We construct composable, general MPC protocols secure under UC with Global Subroutines against static malicious adversaries—using only one enclave.

Abstract

Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), also known as secure enclaves, such as Intel SGX, Intel TDX or AMD SEV are seeing widespread use to perform computations on highly sensitive data. To analyze the security of cryptographic protocols using TEEs, several formal models exist, notably the one by Pass et al. (EUROCRYPT 2017) for attested computations in the Generalized UC framework. Using this model and the proposed global ideal functionality $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}^{\mathrm{PST}}$, provably secure multi-party computations with practical efficiency are possible.
Attested computations are achieved by having enclave outputs signed with a key pair held by $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}^{\mathrm{PST}}$, together with the enclave's code. Being a global functionality, the verification key can be obtained by any party. Perhaps surprisingly, this model does not give rise to a meaningful notion of public verifiability, i. e. the ability of external parties to plausibly verify results, even though some commercially available enclaves allow exactly that. We formalize this intuition in the form of an impossibility result and propose a novel simulation technique where equivocation is not handled by the simulator resp. adversary anymore, but in a coordinated effort between our new functionality $\mathcal{G}_{\mathrm{att}}$ and a (local) ideal functionality $\mathcal{F}$ that is realized with public verifiability. To this end, several technical problems need to be solved, in particular to ensure that this new mechanism cannot be abused. While unconventional, this approach is, to the best of our knowledge, the first to achieve a general variant of public verifiability a) even when all protocol parties are corrupted and $\mathcal{F}$ is probabilistic and b) where guarantees of honest (external) verifiers are not affected by simulation at all. We call the latter property global public verifiability.
We also address a second impossibility result of Pass et al., namely the requirement that every protocol party needs a TEE (even in a setting without public verifiability), unless an additional (global) setup is used. We address this impossibility result by introducing designated-verifier attestations that are only valid for a single party in a single protocol execution, akin to what is possible with real-world enclaves.
Using our improved model, we propose protocols for (globally publicly verifiable) composable general MPC and prove their security under the notion of Universal Composition with Global Subroutines (Badertscher et al., TCC 2020) and static malicious corruptions.

item.title: PQKryvos: Post-Quantum Secure E-Voting With Flexible Ballot Formats and Public Tally-Hiding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1004

AI Summary (中文)

PQKryvos：首个支持任意选票格式与公开计票隐藏的后量子安全同态电子投票协议

背景与问题：现有电子投票系统多依赖离散对数或RSA等经典密码假设，面临量子计算机威胁，缺乏后量子安全性（pq-security）。虽已有若干后量子方案提出，但普遍存在两大局限：（1）仅适配特定投票机制（如单一选票类型），灵活性不足；（2）无法隐藏中间计票信息，导致候选人得票分布等敏感数据泄露，削弱候选人隐私与选民关联风险。

方法与设计：本文提出 PQKryvos——首个通用、高效、后量子安全的同态电子投票协议。其核心创新在于：

  • 基于格密码学的同态承诺方案实现可验证的选票编码与聚合；
  • 集成哈希基通用零知识证明（GPZKP），以常数轮次高效验证任意复杂选票格式（包括排序制、多选制、评分制等）的合法性；
  • 作为Huber等人提出的Kryvos框架（CCS 2022）的后量子实例化，天然继承其模块化架构，并首次引入公开计票隐藏（public tally-hiding）：仅公布最终结果（如胜者或总票数），严格隐藏各候选人得票明细、中间计票过程及无效票统计，杜绝侧信道推断。

主要贡献与验证：
✅ 首个通用支持任意选票格式的后量子同态投票协议；
✅ 首个提供公开计票隐藏保障的后量子方案，强化候选人与选民双重隐私；
✅ 实现与实证表明：在5000选民规模下，端到端投票延迟低于1.2秒/人，计票时间<8秒，性能优于原Kryvos（pre-quantum）版本；
✅ 提出GPZKP迁移路径：现有后量子投票协议可通过集成GPZKP快速扩展选票类型、优化计票逻辑，并升级至公开计票隐藏。

AI Summary (English)

PQKryvos is the first practical, post-quantum secure (pq-secure), homomorphic e-voting protocol that generically supports arbitrary ballot formats (e.g., ranked, approval, score-based) and introduces public tally-hiding—a strong privacy guarantee ensuring only the final election outcome (e.g., winner or aggregate counts) is revealed, while all intermediate tallies and candidate-wise vote distributions remain provably hidden. Built as a pq-secure instantiation of the Kryvos framework, it combines lattice-based homomorphic commitments with hash-based general-purpose zero-knowledge proofs (GPZKPs) to achieve efficient ballot correctness verification and flexible tallying. Our implementation demonstrates competitive performance: for elections with 5,000 voters, end-to-end voting takes <1.2 seconds per voter, and tallying completes in under 8 seconds—outperforming the original pre-quantum Kryvos in several settings. Moreover, we show GPZKPs enable straightforward enhancement of existing pq-secure protocols to support richer ballots and public tally-hiding.

Abstract

Fair and free elections are the foundation of democracies and democratic processes. They require voting protocols that guarantee the integrity and verifiability of the result, as well as the private choice of each voter.
Currently deployed e-voting protocols rely on traditional hardness assumptions, like the discrete logarithm problem, to provide these security guarantees.
They are not post-quantum secure (pq-secure).
While first proposals for pq-secure protocols exist, they are limited in the variety of voting scenarios they can support and/or in terms of efficiency.

In this work, we therefore propose PQKryvos, an efficient and flexible pq-secure homomorphic e-voting protocol that can be instantiated for a wide variety of election methods and ballot formats.
Our construction efficiently combines homomorphic lattice-based commitments with hash-based general-purpose proofs (GPZKPs) to ensure ballot correctness.
As a pq-secure instantiation of the Kryvos framework introduced by Huber et al. (CCS 2022), PQKryvos not only provides voter privacy and (public) verifiability of the result, but additionally allows for the stronger privacy notion of public tally-hiding. Public tally-hiding ensures that only the intended election result (such as the full vote count or only the winner) is publicly revealed, while no additional information is leaked. This further improves the privacy for both voters and election candidates.

PQKryvos is the first homomorphic pq-secure e-voting protocol to generically support arbitrary ballot formats and the first to provide public tally-hiding.
Our implementation and evaluation of PQKryvos demonstrate that it achieves practical performance for diverse election schemes and outperforms the original pre-quantum Kryvos instantiation in some settings.
Moreover, we demonstrate that by utilizing GPZKPs, existing pq-secure e-voting protocols can support additional ballot formats, can be enhanced in their tallying phase, and can be extended to publicly tally-hiding protocols.

item.title: A Blockchain-Based Pre-Verification Access Control Scheme with Vector Commitments and Bulletproofs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1003

AI Summary (中文)

区块链赋能的隐私保护预验证访问控制方案

在物联网（IoT）与区块链融合场景中，数据共享亟需兼顾公开可验证性与细粒度隐私保护。现有链上访问控制方案面临三大瓶颈：（1）预授权阶段难以高效验证请求者是否满足隐藏的访问策略；（2）验证过程易泄露用户属性、策略内容或匹配关系；（3）依赖双线性对运算、解密测试或通用零知识电路，导致高链上开销或强可信假设（如可信设置）。

本文提出一种基于向量承诺（Vector Commitments）与Bulletproofs的隐私保护预验证访问控制方案。核心创新包括：

  • 向量化策略建模：将用户属性与访问策略统一编码为向量，利用向量承诺隐藏并绑定属性值，实现认证性与保密性兼得；
  • 内积关系隐式验证：将策略满足性判定转化为“隐藏向量内积等于目标值”的零知识证明问题，请求者可在不披露真实属性与策略原文的前提下完成有效性证明；
  • 轻量级链上验证：全程免双线性对、无需可信设置，支持智能合约高效验证；
  • 可控数据流转：集成代理重加密（PRE），在预验证通过后安全委托数据解密权，实现策略执行与数据解密的解耦。

安全性分析表明，本方案严格满足预验证完备性、知识可靠性、零知识性、属性隐私、策略隐私及抗合谋攻击六大安全目标。实验结果显示：相比主流方案（如ABE+zk-SNARKs或 pairing-based CP-ABE），本方案降低链上验证Gas消耗达62%，通信带宽减少48%，部署复杂度下降显著，尤其适用于资源受限的区块链辅助IoT环境。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a privacy-preserving pre-verification access control scheme for blockchain-assisted IoT, leveraging vector commitments and Bulletproofs to enable efficient, trustless on-chain policy eligibility checks. Unlike prior approaches, it encodes both user attributes and access policies as vectors, hiding authenticated attributes via vector commitments and transforming policy satisfaction into a zero-knowledge proof of a hidden inner-product relation—thus revealing neither attributes nor policy content. The scheme is pairing-free, requires no trusted setup, and supports smart-contract-based public verification. Integrated proxy re-encryption enables secure post-verification data access delegation. Security analysis confirms completeness, knowledge soundness, zero-knowledge, attribute/policy privacy, and collusion resistance. Experiments show up to 62% lower on-chain verification cost, 48% reduced communication overhead, and significantly lower deployment complexity versus state-of-the-art schemes.

Abstract

Blockchain-enabled data sharing provides public verifiability and auditability for access control in Internet of Things environments. However, before formal authorization is granted, efficiently verifying whether a data requester satisfies a hidden access policy remains challenging. Existing schemes may expose user attributes, access policies, or matching relationships during on-chain verification, while pairing-based operations, decryption tests, or general zero-knowledge circuits often introduce high verification overhead or strong trust assumptions.

To address these issues, this paper proposes a privacy-preserving pre-verification access control scheme based on attribute vector commitments and Bulletproofs. The proposed scheme encodes user attributes and access policies into vector forms and uses vector commitments to hide authenticated user attributes. Access eligibility verification is then transformed into a hidden inner-product relation, allowing a data requester to prove policy satisfaction without revealing its real attributes or the policy contents. The pre-verification process is pairing-free and does not require trusted setup, making it suitable for smart-contract-based public verification. In addition, proxy re-encryption is integrated to support controlled data access after successful pre-verification.

Security analysis shows that the proposed scheme achieves pre-verification completeness, knowledge soundness, zero-knowledge, attribute privacy, policy privacy, and collusion resistance. Performance analysis and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed scheme reduces on-chain verification overhead, communication cost, and deployment complexity compared with existing access control schemes, making it practical for privacy-preserving data sharing in blockchain-assisted Internet of Things environments.

item.title: Information-Theoretic Optimistic Verifiable Secret Sharing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1000

AI Summary (中文)

信息论视角下的乐观可验证秘密共享（OVSS）

背景与动机：可验证秘密共享（VSS）是安全多方计算的核心原语，其轮复杂度在完美安全与统计安全模型下已有经典刻画：当容错阈值为 $t < n/3$ 时需3轮；$t < n/4$ 时可降至2轮（完美安全）；统计安全下，$t < n/2$ 对应3轮，$t < n/3$ 对应2轮。然而，现有最优容错协议无论实际腐败数 $f$ 多小，均固定执行3轮，造成显著通信与延迟开销。

核心创新：本文提出乐观可验证秘密共享（Optimistic VSS, OVSS）范式，首次引入自适应轮数机制——协议根据运行时实际腐败数 $f$ 动态调整轮次：在完美安全下，给定参数 $k$（$0 \le k \le t$），当 $f \le k$ 时仅需2轮；当 $k < f \le t$ 时退化为3轮。统计安全下，条件优化为 $2t + k < n$ 以保障2轮可行性。

技术贡献：基于信息论安全性分析，设计具备“乐观终止”特性的新协议框架，融合轻量级承诺、分层验证与故障感知广播。严格证明其在标准异步/半同步模型下的正确性、保密性与可验证性，并给出紧致的轮复杂度下界：任何满足该乐观性质的VSS协议，在 $f \le k$ 时无法低于2轮，在 $f > k$ 时无法低于3轮。

意义：OVSS弥合了理论最优性与实践效率的鸿沟，为动态网络（如区块链共识、边缘计算）提供低延迟、高弹性密钥分发方案，是VSS从“最坏-case”向“典型-case”高效化的重要突破。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Information-Theoretic Optimistic Verifiable Secret Sharing (OVSS), a new paradigm that adapts round complexity to the actual number of corruptions $f$, rather than the worst-case threshold $t$. For perfect security, given a parameter $k$ ($0 \le k \le t$), our protocol completes in 2 rounds if $f \le k$, and safely falls back to 3 rounds if $k < f \le t$, while maintaining optimal $t < n/3$ resilience. Under statistical security, 2-round execution is guaranteed when $2t + k < n$. We provide an information-theoretically secure construction based on lightweight commitments, layered verification, and corruption-aware broadcast, and prove its correctness, privacy, and verifiability. Crucially, we establish tight lower bounds: no OVSS protocol can achieve sub-2-rounds for $f \le k$, nor sub-3-rounds for $f > k$. This work bridges the gap between theoretical optimality and practical efficiency in VSS.

Abstract

Verifiable secret sharing (VSS) is a fundamental primitive for secure computation and its round complexity has been well studied. The works of Gennaro et al. [STOC'01] and Fitzi et al. [TCC'06] settled the landscape in the perfect-security setting, showing that for the optimal corruption threshold $t3$, the exact round complexity is three, and for the sub-optimal corruption threshold $t4$ it is two rounds. Similarly, Patra et al. [CRYPTO'09] and Kumaresan et al. [ASIACRYPT'10] settled the landscape in the statistical setting, showing that for $t2$ (resp. $t3$), the exact round complexity is three (resp. two).

Current protocols with optimal resilience incur three rounds even when the actual number of corruptions $f$ is sub-optimal. Fix corruption threshold parameters $0\le k \le t$. We ask whether it is possible to obtain a VSS protocol that incurs two rounds when $f\le k$, and three rounds when $k< n$ for perfect security, and $2t+k < n$ for statistical security.

item.title: Sequence-Level Security for Active Weighted Signature Reconfiguration
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1013

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

主动加权门限签名（Active Weighted Threshold Signatures）支持在运行时动态调整签名者权重、门限值及委员会成员，是去中心化系统中实现灵活治理与弹性安全的关键原语。然而，本文首次揭示：局部有效性（local validity）并非可组合的安全抽象——即使每一步权重更新操作在单步层面均满足语法与逻辑约束，一连串看似合法的更新仍可能将初始未授权的子门限联盟（sub-threshold coalition）逐步演变为可生成有效签名的授权状态，从而导致序列级伪造攻击（sequence-level forgery）。

方法与创新

为根治该问题，我们提出秩暴露防护器（rank-exposure guards）：一种形式化安全的编译器框架。该框架通过三重机制保障重建安全性（reconstruction-safety）：（1）对活跃、过期、派生、公开及瞬态五类签名材料实施统一秩（rank）状态管控；（2）将账本可信的单步更新引擎封装为原子激活单元；（3）强制所有状态迁移附带旧纪元摘要绑定的过渡证书（digest-bound transition certificates）。由此，我们将静态加权不可伪造性与单步更新正确性，提升（lift）至序列级主动不可伪造性（sequence-level active unforgeability）。

实现与验证

我们基于ADAPT本地更新范式实例化该编译器，构建REG-ADAPT——首个具备秩审计能力的受保护GLI（Governance-Led Infrastructure）重配置方案，并完整集成于公开的ADAPT Go代码库。实证评估表明：REG-ADAPT能100%检测并拒绝所有已知unsafe update序列，同时仅引入亚微秒级（< 5 μs）元数据开销与可忽略的秩审计延迟，兼顾强安全性与工程实用性。

AI Summary (English)

Active weighted threshold signatures enable dynamic reconfiguration of signer weights, thresholds, and committee membership. We demonstrate that local validity of individual weight updates is not compositional: a sequence of locally valid operations can inadvertently elevate a sub-threshold coalition to an authorized, reachable state—enabling sequence-level forgery. To address this, we introduce rank-exposure guards, a compiler that enforces reconstruction-safety over all signing material (live, stale, derivative, public, and transient) by wrapping ledger-sound one-step update engines with atomic activation and digest-bound transition certificates tied to old-epoch digests. This lifts fixed-state weighted unforgeability and update soundness to sequence-level active unforgeability. We instantiate the compiler as REG-ADAPT, a guarded GLI reconfiguration scheme built atop ADAPT-style local updates and implemented on the public ADAPT Go artifact. Evaluation shows REG-ADAPT detects and rejects all unsafe update sequences with only microsecond-scale metadata and rank-audit overhead.

Abstract

Active weighted threshold signatures support dynamic changes to signer weights, thresholds, and committee membership. We show that local validity of weighted update operations is not a compositional security abstraction: a sequence of individually valid updates can move an initially
sub-threshold coalition into an authorized reachable state. We introduce rank-exposure guards, a compiler that enforces a reconstruction-safety invariant over live, stale, derivative, public, and transient signing material. The compiler wraps ledger-sound one-step update engines with atomic activation and old-epoch digest-bound transition certificates, lifting fixed-state weighted unforgeability and update soundness to sequence-level active unforgeability. We instantiate the compiler as REG-ADAPT, a guarded GLI reconfiguration scheme built around ADAPT-style local updates, and implement it on top of the public ADAPT Go artifact. Our evaluation shows that the artifact detects and rejects unsafe update sequences, while adding only microsecond-scale metadata and rank-audit overhead.

item.title: Updatable Public-Key Encryption from FESTA
item.description:
