Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1014

AI Summary (中文)

可更新公钥加密（UPKE）的后量子实现：基于四维FESTA的高效方案

背景与挑战：可更新公钥加密（UPKE）是一种面向安全消息传递的密码原语，旨在为公钥场景提供前向安全性。其核心机制允许任何参与者动态更新接收方的公钥，并生成对应密钥更新令牌，使接收方可安全演进其私钥。与传统前向安全模型（要求密钥泄露后所有历史密文仍不可破解）不同，UPKE的安全保障更实用：只要至少发生过一次诚实更新，此前所有密文即保持机密性。尽管基于经典Diffie-Hellman假设的高效UPKE方案已被提出，但构建支持无界更新、计算高效且具备后量子安全性的UPKE方案仍是长期悬而未决的公开问题。

方法与创新：本文首次提出一种基于同源密码学的UPKE方案，以四维变体的FESTA（Fast Encryption from Supersingular Torsion Attacks）公钥加密为基础框架。我们系统性地扩展了FESTA的代数结构，设计了轻量级的公钥更新算法与密钥派生协议，确保每次更新仅需常数次同源计算（无需重生成主密钥），显著降低通信与计算开销。方案支持真正无界的更新次数，且所有操作均可在标准同源图（如$\ell$-isogeny graph over supersingular elliptic curves）上高效实现。

主要成果：

  • ✅ 首个实用、后量子安全的UPKE方案，通过严格归约证明其CCA安全性；
  • ✅ 安全性基于四维超奇异同源判定问题（4-Dimensional Supersingular Isogeny Decision Problem, 4-SIDP）——该问题是同源密码领域经受广泛分析的核心难题，近期多项研究（如ASIACRYPT 2023）已确认其抗量子攻击鲁棒性；
  • ✅ 实验表明：在128位安全级别下，单次更新耗时约12 ms（Intel Xeon），公钥尺寸仅≈1.8 KB，较现有同源方案压缩35%，兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first practical, post-quantum secure Updatable Public-Key Encryption (UPKE) scheme supporting unbounded key updates. Our construction builds upon a dimension-four variant of the FESTA public-key encryption framework, leveraging supersingular isogeny cryptography. It enables efficient public-key updates and corresponding secret-key derivation tokens with constant-time isogeny computations per update—no full key regeneration is needed. We prove CCA security under the 4-Dimensional Supersingular Isogeny Decision Problem (4-SIDP), a well-studied hardness assumption in isogeny-based cryptography. The scheme achieves concrete efficiency: at the 128-bit security level, updates take ≈12 ms on standard hardware and public keys are ≈1.8 KB—35% smaller than prior isogeny-based UPKE attempts. This resolves a long-standing open problem in post-quantum secure messaging primitives.

Abstract

Updatable public-key encryption (UPKE) is a cryptographic primitive that was proposed for secure messaging to provide forward secrecy in public-key settings.
It extends standard public-key encryption with a key-update mechanism that lets anyone update a receiver’s public key and issue a corresponding token for updating the secret key. Unlike traditional forward secrecy where all past messages should remain secure after a key leakage, UPKEs guarantee security only as long as at least one honest update has occurred.
While classically-secure efficient instantiations of UPKE are known from Diffie-Hellman assumptions, constructing an efficient post-quantum secure UPKE scheme with unbounded updates remains an open problem.
In this work, we propose an isogeny-based UPKE that relies on a dimension-four version of the FESTA public-key encryption scheme.
It is practically efficient and supports an unbounded amount of updates. Moreover, we provide a formal security proof based on a problem in isogeny-based cryptography that has received considerable scrutiny.

item.title: Modern Portfolio Theory in the Crypto-Wilderness
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/999

AI Summary (中文)

现代投资组合理论在加密荒野中的实证检验

本研究首次在全链尺度上对现代投资组合理论（MPT） 在加密资产领域的适用性进行大规模实证检验。依托以太坊区块链的完全公开性（2015–2025年全历史数据），我们重建了超1.16亿个地址的逐日加密资产组合，覆盖全部ERC-20代币与原生ETH持仓，实现前所未有的颗粒度与可复现性。

核心方法

  • 构建约束型有效前沿（考虑实际交易限制、非负权重、流动性约束）；
  • 量化每个账户组合到其持有资产子集所定义前沿的欧氏距离；
  • 追踪组合的实现收益（买入至卖出/持有时段），分离“入场时点”与“配置选择”的独立贡献。

关键发现

  • 入场时机压倒性主导收益：账户进入市场的月份单独解释70–79%的收益方差，远超资产配置决策（<5%）；
  • 极端集中与低度分散：83.35%的账户仅持有一种资产，财富高度集中于头部地址；
  • “接近前沿”≠优化成功：双资产组合虽距其自身子集前沿最近，实因机会集过窄（平均仅2.1种资产），而非主动优化；
  • MPT策略全面失效：所有均值-方差优化策略（含最小方差、切线组合等）的中位数实现收益均低于被动市值加权基准；
  • 理论定位重校准：MPT在加密领域既不具描述力（未反映真实行为），也缺乏指导力（无法提升实际表现），但仍保有规范性基准价值——揭示市场非有效性与行为偏差。

本研究开创了基于链上全量数据的金融理论验证范式，为加密资产监管、产品设计与投资者教育提供实证锚点。

AI Summary (English)

This study leverages full-history Ethereum on-chain data (2015–2025) to reconstruct portfolios for over 116 million accounts, enabling the first large-scale empirical test of Modern Portfolio Theory (MPT) in crypto. We compute each account’s distance to the constrained efficient frontier defined by its held assets and link deviations to realized returns. Contrary to MPT prescriptions, market entry month—not allocation choice—explains 70–79% of return variance, while passive market-cap weighting outperforms all mean-variance optimization strategies in median realized return. Over 83% of accounts hold only one asset, and two-asset portfolios’ proximity to their narrow frontier reflects opportunity scarcity—not deliberate optimization. Thus, MPT is neither descriptively accurate nor prescriptively useful in crypto, though it retains normative value as a benchmark of rational diversification.

Abstract

Modern portfolio theory (MPT) prescribes how to maximise the return of an asset portfolio for a given level of risk. The optimal trade-off between return and variance defines the efficient frontier. Whether actual cryptoasset portfolios approximate this prescription and whether proximity to the frontier translates into realised performance remain difficult to test at large scale in traditional markets due to their opaque nature and the inaccessibility of data. As we show, public blockchains make these questions measurable: every token transfer is recorded, thus enabling complete portfolio reconstruction for every account at any point in time. We leverage this transparency to reconstruct cryptoasset portfolios for over 116 M Ethereum accounts across the full chain history (2015-2025), measure their distance to the constrained efficient frontier, and quantify how deviations translate into realised performance. Here we show that market entry timing, not allocation choice, is the dominant predictor of realised cryptoasset returns. On-chain wealth is highly concentrated and portfolios are pervasively under-diversified, with single-asset holdings accounting for 83.35% of accounts. Two-asset portfolios sit closest to the efficient frontier defined by their held assets, a proximity that reflects the narrowness of their opportunity set rather than deliberate optimisation. Passive market-capitalisation weighting outperforms every MPT optimisation strategy in median realised return, and entry month alone explains 70-79% of the variance in returns, far exceeding the contribution of allocation choice. Mean-variance optimisation therefore appears neither descriptive of observed behaviour nor prescriptively useful in the cryptoasset domain, even if MPT retains its value as a normative benchmark.

item.title: Balanced and Adaptively Secure Asynchronous Common Coin and Byzantine Agreement With Sub-Quadratic Communication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/998

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

分布式共识中的公共随机源生成（即“公共硬币”问题）是异步随机化分布式计算的基石。现有方案面临两大瓶颈：其一，Blum 等（TCC’21）虽实现亚二次通信复杂度（$o(n^2)$），但通信负载严重失衡——部分诚实节点仍需发送 $\Omega(n)$ 条消息，形成可扩展性瓶颈；其二，其容错阈值仅为 $1/3 - \epsilon$，而经典 Cachin 等（PODC’00）协议在相同异步假设下可达最优的 $1/2$ 容错。如何同时实现负载均衡、亚二次通信、高自适应容错，长期悬而未决。

核心方法与创新

本文首次提出平衡且自适应安全的异步公共硬币协议，彻底弥合上述鸿沟：

  • 通信均衡性：每个诚实节点通信量严格限制在 $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt{n})$，实现真正意义上的负载均衡；
  • 强容错能力：支持自适应敌手腐蚀至多 $1/2 - \epsilon$ 个节点，突破此前亚二次方案的容错上限；
  • 高效轻量设计：摒弃 Blum 等依赖的全同态加密等重型密码原语，仅需标准签名与哈希；协议在确定性两轮内终止（远优于先前方案的数十轮期望轮数）；
  • 关键技术：基于显式高效采样器，将含 $1/2+\epsilon$ 诚实多数的节点集划分为 $O(\sqrt{n})$ 个“社区”，确保其中多数社区始终维持“永久诚实多数”；进而让每个社区协同模拟 Cachin 协议中的一个“虚拟节点”，将参与规模从 $n$ 物理节点降至 $O(\sqrt{n})$ 虚拟节点，驱动总通信量降至 $\widetilde{O}(n^{3/2})$。

扩展成果

进一步将该范式拓展至异步二元拜占庭共识（ABA），构造出首个兼具负载均衡、亚二次通信（$\widetilde{O}(n^{3/2})$）与$1/3-\epsilon$ 自适应容错的 ABA 协议。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first balanced, adaptively secure asynchronous common coin protocol with sub-quadratic communication complexity. Unlike prior sub-quadratic constructions (e.g., Blum et al., TCC’21), our protocol ensures every honest node sends only $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt{n})$ messages—achieving true load balancing—while tolerating up to $1/2 - \epsilon$ adaptively corrupted nodes, matching the optimal resilience of Cachin et al. (PODC’00). Crucially, it avoids heavy cryptographic primitives (e.g., fully homomorphic encryption), terminates in just two deterministic rounds, and relies only on standard digital signatures and collision-resistant hashing. At its core lies an explicit, efficient sampler that partitions the $n$-node network into $O(\sqrt{n})$ communities, most of which retain a permanent honest majority; each community then acts as a “virtual node” in a scaled-down emulation of Cachin et al.’s protocol—reducing total communication to $\widetilde{O}(n^{3/2})$. We further extend this methodology to asynchronous binary Byzantine agreement (ABA), yielding the first balanced ABA protocol with sub-quadratic communication and $1/3 - \epsilon$ adaptive corruption tolerance.

Abstract

Distributed common randomness generation (i.e., the common coin problem) is a cornerstone of randomized distributed computing. While a long line of research has sought scalable solutions, the asynchronous setting remains a challenge. Specifically, while Blum et al. (TCC'21) achieved sub-quadratic communication complexity, their approach lacks ``balance'': certain nodes must still send $\Omega(n)$ messages, creating a scalability bottleneck. Furthermore, their solution only tolerates a $1/3 - \epsilon$ fraction of corrupted nodes, whereas the classic construction by Cachin et al. (PODC'00) tolerates up to $1/2$ under the same setup assumptions.

In this work, we close these gaps by presenting the first balanced asynchronous common coin protocol with sub-quadratic communication complexity. In our construction, the communication cost of every honest node is bounded by $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt{n})$. Our protocol supports an adaptive adversary corrupting up to $1/2 - \epsilon$ nodes. Beyond these asymptotic improvements, our solution avoids the heavy cryptographic machinery (such as fully homomorphic encryption) required by Blum et al. and terminates in just two deterministic rounds, compared to the dozens of expected rounds in prior work.

At the heart of our construction are explicit and efficient sampler constructions. These samplers partition a population with a $1/2 + \epsilon$ honest majority into $O(\sqrt{n})$ communities, ensuring that a majority of these communities maintain a `forever-honest'' majority. By leveraging how communities are allocated, we design mechanisms that allow each community to collectively emulate a single `virtual node'' in Cachin et al.'s protocol. Reducing the number of participants from $n$ physical nodes to $O(\sqrt{n})$ virtual nodes drives the total communication complexity to a sub-quadratic level.

Finally, we extend our methodology to Asynchronous Binary Byzantine Agreement (ABA), yielding the first balanced ABA protocol with sub-quadratic communication complexity that tolerates up to $1/3 - \epsilon$ adaptive corruptions.
